summary: Design the co-trained M3 (oscillator+hypernet end-to-end) to bypass forgetting — proposals, adversarial critique, synthesis into a buildable pre-registered plan

proposals.name: PALM: Phase-Attractor Ladder Memory (grown Kuramoto bias-anchors + energy-based label-free attractor selection, co-trained with the hypernet)
proposals.core_idea: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J ONCE (on task 1, then freeze it as the shared binding substrate), and give each new task its OWN learnable "attractor anchor": a small bias field b_t injected into the KLayer conditional-stimulus c at the context layer L1 (the existing `c = self.c_norm(c)` injection point in kupdate, dxdt = omg_x + project(connectivity(x)+c, x)). Anchor b_t reshapes the Kuramoto energy landscape so inputs from task t settle into a DISTINCT stable phase fixed-point (a Hopfield-style attractor realized inside Kuramoto dynamics); the pooled phase of that fixed-point is the context c_phase fed to the hypernet g, which generates the head theta for the frozen-random trunk. Anchors are GROWN one-per-task and old anchors are frozen, so adding task B literally cannot overwrite task A's attractor (anti-forgetting = parameter-disjoint attractor banks + a frozen shared landscape). At test we run the recurrence under every candidate anchor and pick the one yielding the lowest Kuramoto energy (= the input synchronized best into that basin), so phase self-selects context with no task id.
proposals.what_is_trained_vs_frozen: FROZEN always: the random trunk (C1 unbypassability) and omega/natural-frequency terms. SHARED-then-FROZEN: the coupling J (KLayer.connectivity) and c_norm/gamma are co-trained end-to-end with g during TASK 1 ONLY (so the binding machinery is learned, satisfying the co-training bet and beating R6s where J is random), then J is frozen for all t>=2. TRAINED per task t: (a) a new attractor anchor b_t — a low-rank bias field of shape (n*G,) broadcast over H,W at L1, ~a few hundred params, injected additively into c; (b) a new hypernet "column" g_t (a small adapter: one extra hidden row in fc1 plus its fc2 fan-out, or a per-anchor low-rank delta to g's weights). When training task t, b_1..b_{t-1} and g_1..g_{t-1} are FROZEN (requires_grad_(False)); only b_t, g_t, and a tiny shared anchor-gate train. This is the AKOrN realization of progressive/expandable nets: the substrate (J) is shared and frozen, capacity grows by parameter-disjoint phase attractors.
proposals.losses: (1) CE on current task: L_cls = CrossEntropy(g_t(c_phase(x;b_t)) applied to trunk(x), y), the only signal during task 1 that also trains J. (2) Attractor-formation loss (the Hopfield-x-Kuramoto term), applied every task: L_attr = lambda_e * E_kur(x; b_t) for in-task x, where E_kur is the negative tangent-space synchrony of the converged L1 state (already computable as `sim` from project()); this DRIVES task-t inputs to a deep, well-synchronized fixed point under b_t. (3) Attractor-separation/margin loss, applied every task t>=2: L_sep = lambda_s * relu(margin - (E_kur(x; b_j) - E_kur(x; b_t))) summed over a few FROZEN past anchors j<t on a small replay buffer of past inputs (200 stored images total) — pushes the energy under the wrong/old anchor ABOVE the right one so the energy-argmin selector is correct. (4) Anchor-stability loss (the C3 crux), every task t>=2: L_stab = lambda_c * mean_{j<t} ( E_kur(x_j^replay; b_j) - E_kur^*(j) )^2, where E_kur^*(j) is the energy each old attractor had right after task j (a stored scalar per anchor); since b_j is frozen this only re-checks that the SHARED frozen landscape + new anchor haven't perturbed old basins. lambda_e=1.0, lambda_s=0.5, margin=0.1, lambda_c=0.5 (one pre-set, no shopping).
proposals.cl_protocol: Sequential class-IL over the 5-task shapes construct (or SplitCIFAR-10), using shp._experiences. Task 1: co-train {J, c_norm, gamma, b_1, g_1} with L_cls+L_attr to convergence; then HARD-FREEZE J, c_norm, gamma forever; record E_kur^*(1) and store ~40 task-1 exemplars in the anchor-replay buffer. Task boundary t (t>=2): (i) instantiate new anchor b_t (init from the centroid of current-task converged phases) and new hypernet column g_t; (ii) freeze all old b_j, g_j; (iii) train {b_t, g_t} with L_cls + L_attr + L_sep + L_stab, interleaving the small replay buffer ONLY for the energy losses (L_sep/L_stab use stored exemplars; L_cls uses current data + optionally replayed labels); (iv) record E_kur^*(t), add ~40 task-t exemplars to the buffer (cap 200, reservoir). At each boundary the only NEW trainable params are one anchor (~hundreds) + one g-column, so total growth over 5 tasks is negligible vs the frozen trunk/J.
proposals.how_it_stays_label_free_at_test: At inference, no task id is given. For input x: run the L1 Kuramoto recurrence ONCE per stored anchor b_1..b_T (T forwards through the cheap recurrent core, trunk computed once), and for each compute the converged-state Kuramoto energy E_kur(x; b_j) (the `sim` synchrony scalar, pooled). SELECT j* = argmin_j E_kur (the basin the input synchronizes into best — the energy-based "which attractor did this settle to"), then use c_phase under b_{j*} and head g_{j*} to classify. This is exactly how a Hopfield net retrieves a pattern (lowest-energy basin) but realized in Kuramoto phase space, and it requires only the inputs. The separation loss L_sep is what makes this selector reliable. A soft variant (softmax over -E_kur as attention weights to mix anchors/heads) is a fallback if hard argmin is brittle, and degrades gracefully.
proposals.anti_forgetting_mechanism: Three concrete, stacked guarantees that the oscillator cannot forget task A's phase region when learning task B (C3): (1) STRUCTURAL/PARAMETER-DISJOINT: old anchors b_j and old heads g_j have requires_grad_(False) during task t; the gradient of any task-t loss w.r.t. them is never applied — task B's optimizer physically cannot move task A's attractor parameters. (2) FROZEN SHARED LANDSCAPE: the coupling J (the part shared across attractors, whose drift WOULD couple basins) is frozen after task 1, so the global energy surface that defines all basins is fixed; only additive, localized anchor biases are added on top. This is the key difference from naive co-training, where J keeps moving and re-sculpts old basins. (3) ENERGY-ANCHORED CONSISTENCY: L_stab explicitly penalizes any change in the converged energy of each old attractor on its stored exemplars (target = the energy snapshot E_kur^*(j) taken right after task j), catching any residual cross-basin leakage through the shared substrate (e.g. via the selector or the small shared anchor-gate) and pinning old basins in place. So forgetting is blocked at the parameter level (1,2) AND functionally regularized (3).
proposals.why_it_closes_the_gap: M2 proved the limit is the PHASE ITSELF, not the readout: a captured/frozen phase channel + better readout was null, and the channel's task-level capacity caps at ~0.24 because synchrony binds objects within an image but does not natively encode WHICH task (the S_N symmetry barrier). PALM directly breaks that symmetry by SHAPING the phase: each task gets a dedicated learned attractor that forces task-distinct fixed points, so the context the hypernet sees is task-separable by construction rather than by accident — moving toward the oracle one-hot regime (~0.52) precisely because the energy-argmin over well-separated anchors approximates a recovered task id, but derived label-free from dynamics. Crucially this is the part the failed contrastive-on-frozen-phase experiment could not do: it shaped the readout, PALM shapes the dynamics' attractors. Because old anchors/heads are frozen, the per-task accuracy learned at task t is retained (forgetting near 0), so final_acc -> mean of per-task learn_acc rather than collapsing to chance (~0.13 currently). The realistic target is final_acc ~0.40-0.50 with low forgetting: bounded above by the oracle 0.52 and by selector error.
proposals.main_failure_mode: Attractor-selection collapse / basin overlap: if the energy gaps between anchors are small (E_kur under wrong anchors ~ under the right one), the label-free argmin selector mis-routes inputs at test, so even with frozen perfectly-retained heads the accuracy is bottlenecked by selector error (and could fall toward R6s if J-as-random can't form deep distinct basins). This is the single biggest risk and is exactly what L_sep targets; it is measurable directly as "selector accuracy" (fraction of test inputs whose energy-argmin equals true task) and decoupled from head accuracy. Secondary risk: a degenerate bypass where the anchor b_t leaks label info so g_t ignores phase — caught by the constant-context / wrong-context dP5 invariants already in the harness (must still drop) and by checking selector accuracy >> chance with R6 >> R6s.
proposals.cheapest_validation_milestone: GO/NO-GO in ~1-2 GPU-hours on the 5-task shapes construct, 1 seed: train task 1 (co-train J + b_1 + g_1), freeze J, then ADD only b_2 (grow one anchor, freeze b_1/g_1) for task 2. Measure TWO numbers: (a) SELECTOR ACCURACY — for held-out task-1 vs task-2 inputs, does energy-argmin over {b_1,b_2} pick the right basin? GO if >= ~0.75 (chance 0.5) for R6 AND R6 beats R6s (random J) by a clear margin; (b) RETENTION — after adding b_2, is task-1 accuracy (via frozen b_1,g_1) unchanged vs right after task 1 (delta < ~0.02)? This isolates the two crux claims (label-free attractor separation forms, and growing+freezing prevents forgetting) on the smallest possible 2-task slice before committing to the full 5-task/CIFAR run. NO-GO if selector ~chance or task-1 drops — means basins overlap and the L_sep/L_stab weights or anchor rank need rethinking before scaling.

proposals.name: PALACE: Phase-Anchored Low-rank Coupling with EMA consolidation (two-timescale oscillator hypernet)
proposals.core_idea: Co-train the AKOrN coupling and the hypernet g end-to-end, but separate two timescales AND make the slow part STRUCTURALLY task-local instead of merely slow. The shared backbone coupling J0 (klayer.connectivity, all L layers) stays FROZEN-random (it is the symmetry-respecting "object-binding" prior). Task adaptation of the oscillator flows through two small, slow, per-task structural knobs injected at the kupdate bias point c: (1) a learned low-rank coupling delta dJ_t = U_t V_t^T (rank r~4, added to the L1 connectivity output) and (2) an explicit symmetry-breaking phase drive b_t (a learned C-dim additive bias to c) -- these are the "slow protected variables." The hypernet g (fast) maps the resulting pooled L1 phase context to the head weights as in M2. The crux fix vs the seed: at TEST there is no task id, so a tiny label-free ROUTER reads the unsupervised Kuramoto order parameter of the FROZEN-J0 first pass and soft-selects which slow knob set {dJ_t, b_t} to inject; a second oscillator pass with that knob produces a task-separated phase region that g can decode. Slowness alone does not protect old regions (a slow global J still drifts B-ward); task-LOCAL knobs + consolidation do.
proposals.what_is_trained_vs_frozen: FROZEN (C1 unbypassability): the frozen-random trunk (trunk in _build_pieces) feeding features f; the backbone coupling J0 = all klayer.connectivity convs and conv0/transition/readout of the AKOrN (kept at random init, requires_grad_(False)); omega. So the ONLY task-adaptive path to f->logits is phase->g. TRAINED-SLOW (low LR 1e-4, EMA-consolidated, one set per task slot K=n_tasks): the low-rank coupling deltas {U_t (C x r), V_t (C x r)} added to L1 connectivity, the symmetry-breaking drives {b_t (C-vector)} added to the L1 bias c, and the SI/Fisher importance buffers. TRAINED-FAST (LR 1e-3): the hypernet g (PhaseContextThetaGen) exactly as M2, and the label-free router R (a 2-layer MLP from a small rotation-invariant order-parameter descriptor -> K soft weights). At a task boundary the just-finished slot t is EMA-frozen (its dJ_t,b_t LR -> 0, copied to an EMA shadow used at test). Param budget added over M2: ~K*(2Cr + C) + tiny router = ~5*(2*64*4+64)+~5k, trivial.
proposals.losses: Per training step on current task t (labels y available, class-incremental): (L1) CE = crossentropy(g(phase_ctx_t)·f, y) -- the M2 task loss, drives g + the slow knobs jointly. (L2) phase-separation hinge L_sep = mean_{t'<t} relu(m - ||mu_t - mu_t'||^2) on the pooled phase-context centroids, m~1.0, weight 0.3 -- pushes task t's phase region away from CONSOLIDATED old centroids (old mu_t' are EMA constants, no grad to old knobs); this is the explicit S_N symmetry-breaker the project found missing (NOT contrastive-on-frozen, which was null -- here it shapes the LIVE phase via dJ_t,b_t). (L3) router supervision L_route = crossentropy(R(orderparam(x)), t) using the CURRENT task id during training only (legit class-IL; label-free at test). (L4) anti-forget consolidation L_si = sum_i Omega_i (p_i - p_i*)^2 over slow params (SI/online-EWC) applied from task 2 on, weight lambda~50, with importances Omega accumulated over training. (L5) phase-anchor replay: tiny per-task buffer of the FROZEN J0 first-pass order-param descriptors + 8 raw exemplars/task; replay re-runs the full two-pass forward and adds CE+L_sep so old basins are actively re-pinned. Total = L1 + 0.3 L2 + L3 + 50 L4 + replay(L1+0.3 L2).
proposals.cl_protocol: Sequential over the 5 shapes-pair experiences (or SplitCIFAR-10), naive class-IL order. WITHIN task t: pass-1 = run AKOrN with FROZEN J0 only (no knobs) to get an unsupervised order-parameter descriptor; train router R toward id t (L3) and read its argmax/soft weights w; pass-2 = re-run L1 oscillator with injected knob = sum_k w_k (dJ_k applied in connectivity, b_k added to c) -- during training force w to the true one-hot of t (teacher-forced slot) so dJ_t,b_t get clean gradient; pool L1 phase -> ctx -> g -> head; backprop L1+L2 into {dJ_t,b_t,g,router}, L4 into slow params. AT TASK BOUNDARY t->t+1: (a) snapshot mu_t (mean phase-context over task t) into the frozen centroid set used by L2; (b) EMA-consolidate dJ_t,b_t into shadow copies and set their LR to ~0 (slow protection); (c) update SI importances Omega for slow params; (d) add 8 exemplars + their pass-1 descriptors to the phase-anchor buffer. g and router keep training across all tasks (they are the fast/shared shared learners, protected by L4/replay).
proposals.how_it_stays_label_free_at_test: No teacher forcing at test. Test forward: pass-1 runs the FROZEN-J0 oscillator and computes a rotation/permutation-robust order-parameter descriptor (per-group coherence eigenvalues of the group-direction Gram + global Kuramoto R = |mean phase vector| -- all S_N/rotation invariant, so it depends on input structure not labels); the router R maps it to soft weights w over the K consolidated knob slots; pass-2 injects the soft-mixed slow knob sum_k w_k{dJ_k,b_k}; the resulting phase self-organizes into the consolidated task region; g decodes it to a head. Phase thus self-organizes to context from the INPUT alone -- the router is the label-free selector, and because each slot's knob was consolidated to a distinct phase region (L2) the soft-max sharpens to the right basin. C2 satisfied: the only thing that ever sees a task id is the router's TRAINING target (allowed in class-IL); inference uses none.
proposals.anti_forgetting_mechanism: This is the C3 crux and is attacked at THREE structurally independent levels so phase regions survive sequential training. (1) STRUCTURAL LOCALITY: task t's oscillator adaptation lives ONLY in its own slot {dJ_t,b_t}; learning task B writes to slot B, never to slot A's parameters -- so A's phase-generating dynamics are literally untouched by B's gradients (this is why slot-local low-rank deltas beat a single slow global J, which the seed proposes: a slow global J still drifts and carries ALL old regions with it). (2) CONSOLIDATION: once a slot is EMA-frozen at its boundary its LR->0 and an SI/online-EWC penalty (L4) pins it; the router R and hypernet g (which ARE shared and could forget) are held by L4 on their important units + by replay. (3) ACTIVE BASIN RE-PINNING: the phase-anchor buffer replays old tasks' pass-1 descriptors and exemplars every task, re-running the full two-pass forward so g and the router are re-exposed to old phase regions and L_sep keeps centroids separated -- this directly tests and corrects the failure where co-training collapses old basins. The oracle's 0.02 forgetting shows: if the knob reliably reconstructs the right phase region, g+head don't forget; PALACE makes the knob non-overwritable (level 1) and the router's selection re-pinned (levels 2-3).
proposals.why_it_closes_the_gap: The measured limiter is task capacity of the phase channel: within-task-shuffle ~0.24 vs oracle ~0.52 with the SAME g+head+trunk. The oracle wins purely because its 2n context is cleanly task-separated. PALACE makes the LIVE phase approach the oracle's separability without a label: the low-rank dJ_t + symmetry-breaking drive b_t are an explicit, trainable symmetry-breaker (the S_N-barrier fix Verbeke&Verguts predicted) that pushes each task's phase region apart (enforced by L_sep on live, co-trained phase -- distinct from the null contrastive-on-FROZEN-phase probe, which couldn't move frozen phase). As phase separation rises, the within-shuffle task lift rises toward oracle, and the router converts that separation into the correct slot at test. So the channel's effective C_ctx grows toward H(T) from the input side (better phase) rather than from a better readout (already shown insufficient). Plausible path: 0.24 -> mid-0.3s/0.4s as L_sep widens the regions; the residual gap to 0.52 is router confusability, measurable directly.
proposals.main_failure_mode: Router collapse / basin confusion at test: if the frozen-J0 order-parameter descriptor is not itself task-discriminative (the same S_N barrier that made frozen phase weakly task-decodable -- linear AND nonlinear probes were null at n=17), the router cannot pick the right slot label-free, soft weights smear, the injected knob is an averaged mush, phase lands between basins, and accuracy regresses toward the naive ~0.13 collapse. In short: PALACE can make the phase regions separable GIVEN the right knob, but selecting the knob without a label still rides on the same weak pass-1 signal. Secondary risk: the router/L_route learns an instance-leak shortcut (selecting on exemplar identity, the 54% instance component we found) rather than task structure, inflating train acc but failing the within-task-shuffle and wrong-context dP5 controls. Mitigation/honesty: the cheapest milestone below directly measures router slot-accuracy as the go/no-go.
proposals.cheapest_validation_milestone: ~1-2 GPU-hours on the existing shapes-5-task harness, n=3 seeds, R6 vs R6s, measuring the ONE quantity the whole design rides on: pass-1 ROUTER SLOT-ACCURACY = can the label-free router R, reading only the frozen-J0 order-parameter descriptor, recover the true task slot on held-out test inputs (chance=0.20)? Implement minimally: add slot params {dJ_t (rank-4),b_t}, train them + g JOINTLY but NON-sequentially (joint upper bound, reuse run_joint plumbing) with L1+0.3*L_sep, then freeze and evaluate (a) router slot-acc, (b) within-task-shuffle task acc vs the current 0.24 and vs oracle 0.52. GO if router slot-acc >> 0.20 (say >=0.45) AND within-shuffle task acc rises >= +0.08 over 0.24 for R6, with R6 beating R6s (C4). NO-GO (kills it cheaply, before any sequential/C3 build) if router slot-acc ~ chance -- that means the label-free selection signal isn't there and the sequential consolidation machinery would be protecting an unusable channel. This isolates the single deciding risk (label-free slot selection) for the smallest possible spend.

proposals.name: PhaseLock: co-trained phase-region locking with prototype-anchored contrastive separation and dual-replay
proposals.core_idea: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J (and omega g_omg) jointly with the hypernet g so the layer-1 phase-context c(x) self-organizes into near-orthogonal per-task clusters that g can route to disjoint parameter regions, mimicking the oracle's one-hot contexts (which hit final~0.53, forgetting~0.00). Three coupled forces during sequential training: (1) a supervised-contrastive loss on the DIFFERENTIABLE phase-context pushing same-task contexts together and different-task contexts apart (toward a frozen orthogonal target frame), applied online while the task is current; (2) a per-task phase PROTOTYPE p_t (the running EMA of c over task t) plus an elastic "phase-region lock" that, when learning task B, penalizes any drift of c(x_A) away from its stored prototype for replayed/anchor inputs of past tasks A -- this is what stops J from overwriting task A's phase region (the C3 hazard); (3) dual low-dim replay: a tiny buffer of (raw-input or stored phase-context, feature, label) interleaved to stabilize g, plus the prototypes themselves replayed as synthetic contexts so g never loses the c->theta map for old clusters even if no raw exemplars survive. The diagnosis from the existing logs is precise: frozen-phase replay collapses dP5 to ~0 (g learns to ignore the phase and overwrite one shared head); the oracle keeps dP5~-0.53. PhaseLock's job is to keep dP5 strongly negative THROUGHOUT the stream by making and KEEPING the phase clusters separable.
proposals.what_is_trained_vs_frozen: TRAINED: (a) the AKOrN coupling J = each KLayer.connectivity conv (R6 arm; in R6s arm these stay frozen-random as the C4 control), (b) the AKOrN omega params (use_omega/learn_omg already on), (c) the hypernet g = PhaseContextThetaGen (HyperHead.gen). FROZEN, permanently: the random task-agnostic trunk (the nn.Sequential conv stack feeding feat_dim=64 features to the generated head) -- this preserves C1 unbypassability, the phase->theta path remains the only task-adaptive route. NOT TRAINABLE buffers (updated by EMA, no grad): per-task phase prototypes p_t (2n=8-dim), the frozen orthogonal target frame E (T rows in R^{2n}, e.g. a fixed random-orthonormal or one-hot-padded set), and the small replay buffer. KEY HARNESS CHANGE: replace the numpy/detached _phase_context with a Torch-differentiable port of group_directions+_pool_phase_state (reshape (B,C//n,n,H,W), L2-normalize over the n-axis with eps, then concat mean and mean-square over the site axis) so gradients flow c->J. The AKOrN is NO LONGER frozen before the CL loop (the current run_cl_reg pre-trains then freezes); instead J trains jointly inside the per-task loop. The brief AKOrN classification pre-train (ctx_epochs) is kept ONLY as initialization so phase starts meaningful.
proposals.losses: Per current task t, per minibatch of current-task inputs x_b (labels y_b): L = L_cls + lambda_sc*L_supcon + lambda_lock*L_lock + lambda_rep*L_replay + lambda_proto*L_proto. (1) L_cls = CE(g(c(x_b)) applied to trunk(x_b), y_b) -- standard class-IL supervised signal (C2-legal: uses current labels at train time only). (2) L_supcon = supervised-contrastive (Khosla) on the differentiable phase-context c(x_b) using TASK-ID as the positive key, with an anchoring term pulling each task's mean context toward its assigned row E[t] of the frozen orthogonal frame: temperature 0.1, applied EVERY current-task batch (this is the 'during-CL, not post-hoc on frozen phase' fix). (3) L_lock = sum over a replay/anchor minibatch of past tasks s<t of ||c(x_s) - p_s||^2 (elastic phase-region lock: keep old inputs mapping to their established phase prototype -- directly addresses C3). (4) L_replay = CE on replayed (feature, label) with their STORED phase-context (or re-computed c(x_s)) so g keeps the old c->theta maps; small buffer (~k per class). (5) L_proto = CE on SYNTHETIC contexts sampled as p_s + small gaussian jitter with the stored majority labels per (task,class), so g is regularized on the old clusters even when raw exemplars are scarce. Defaults to validate: lambda_sc=0.5, lambda_lock=1.0, lambda_proto=0.3, lambda_rep=1.0, betas swept on the cheap milestone. After each task: update p_t = EMA of c over task-t inputs; freeze p_t.
proposals.cl_protocol: 5-task shapes (10-way, 2 classes/task) or SplitCIFAR-10 via existing shp._experiences. INIT: brief joint pre-train of AKOrN on task-0 data only (ctx_epochs, init so phase is non-trivial) -- do NOT freeze it. PER TASK t = 0..T-1: train J + omega + g jointly for `epochs` with L above over current-task batches interleaved with replay/anchor batches from buffer (tasks <t). AT EACH TASK BOUNDARY (entering t): (a) freeze nothing new; (b) compute and store prototype p_{t-1} = EMA mean of differentiable c over all just-finished task inputs (and per-class sub-prototypes for L_proto); (c) reserve E[t] (next orthogonal target row) for the incoming task -- assigned by current task id, available at train time per C2; (d) push k exemplars per class of the finished task into the replay buffer (store raw x OR the 8-dim c + 64-dim feature + label -- low-dim, the SEED's 'low-dim phase-context replay'). At TEST: no task id; c(x) computed from input alone, g(c(x)) generates the head, applied to trunk(x). Report the full forgetting matrix, final_acc, forgetting, and dP5 (wrong-context swap) exactly as run_cl_reg already does, vs oracle (~0.52 ceiling) and vs R6s.
proposals.how_it_stays_label_free_at_test: Task ids are used ONLY during training: to define supcon positives, to pick the orthogonal target row E[t], and to index prototypes p_t. At inference the phase c(x) is produced by the (now co-trained) AKOrN from the raw input alone -- exactly the frozen forward path the harness already uses, minus the freeze. The supcon+orthogonal-frame training MAKES the dynamics route each task's inputs into its own basin: because J was shaped so that task-t inputs converge to phases near E[t], a fresh test input self-selects its cluster by which basin its phase falls into, and g (trained to map that cluster -> task-t head) emits the right parameters with no id supplied. The dP5 wrong-context swap is the falsifier: if c truly self-organizes to context, swapping in another task's context must crater accuracy (dP5 strongly negative, like the oracle's -0.53), proving the routing is phase-driven and label-free, not a memorized id.
proposals.anti_forgetting_mechanism: C3 is the crux: when J trains on task B it can drag task A's inputs out of their phase basin, silently destroying the c->theta map g built for A. PhaseLock defends the OSCILLATOR'S OWN phase regions with three layers. (1) PHASE-REGION LOCK (primary): L_lock = ||c(x_A) - p_A||^2 on replayed/anchor inputs of every past task, computed through the live differentiable AKOrN -- this is an EWC-style elastic anchor but in PHASE space on J, not weight space, so it explicitly forbids J from moving old inputs off their frozen prototypes. The prototypes p_A are EMA-frozen at A's boundary, giving a fixed target the new task cannot renegotiate. (2) ORTHOGONAL TARGET FRAME: by binding each task to a pre-reserved near-orthogonal row E[t], new tasks are pushed into UNOCCUPIED phase directions rather than colliding with old clusters -- it geometrically reduces the overwrite pressure before the lock even acts. (3) PROTOTYPE REPLAY into g (L_proto + L_replay): even if J drifts slightly, g keeps a correct head for cluster A because it is continuously retrained on synthetic contexts drawn around p_A with A's labels, so g cannot collapse old clusters to the shared overwriting head that killed the frozen-phase runs (dP5->0). The combination locks BOTH the oscillator's phase placement (so c(x_A) stays put) AND g's readout of it (so theta(p_A) stays correct) -- attacking the exact failure the logs show.
proposals.why_it_closes_the_gap: The logs pin the gap to ONE quantity: dP5. Oracle contexts are orthogonal (one-hot) -> g carves disjoint param regions -> dP5~-0.53, final~0.53, forgetting~0.00. Frozen-phase R6 contexts overlap and are not protected -> during CL g abandons the phase channel (dP5 collapses to ~-0.01) and overwrites a single shared head -> final~0.13, forgetting~0.5-0.7. The post-hoc supcon readout failed because it reshaped the READOUT, not the phase; M2 proved a better readout on frozen phase is not enough. PhaseLock shapes the PHASE ITSELF (trains J) to be orthogonal-clustered like the oracle (driving dP5 toward -0.5) AND protects those clusters under sequential training, so g can and does use disjoint parameter regions per task. Capacity argument (C_ctx>=H(T)): the supcon+orthogonal-frame loss is exactly maximizing the mutual information between context cluster and task, raising C_ctx from the modest task-only ~0.24 toward the H(T)=log2(5)~2.3 bits the oracle achieves. If J can be shaped to separate 5 clusters as well as one-hot (a far easier ask than the original classification, since we only need 5 separable basins, not 10-way accuracy), final accuracy should climb from ~0.13/0.24 toward the ~0.52 ceiling.
proposals.main_failure_mode: The supcon/orthogonal-frame pressure on J may fight the L_cls signal: to make phases task-separable J might homogenize WITHIN-task structure that the trunk+head still needs for class discrimination, capping per-task learn_acc below oracle's ~0.53 (a separability-vs-discriminability tension). Symptom: dP5 goes strongly negative (clusters separate) but learn_acc drops -- the phase becomes a clean task flag but loses the residual class info, so we get oracle-like routing with sub-oracle within-task accuracy. Second-order risk: the phase-region lock is only as good as the anchor coverage; if the buffer is too small or prototypes too coarse (8-dim is low), J can satisfy L_lock on anchors while still drifting non-anchor task-A inputs, partially reviving C3. Cheating risk handled by C4 controls: must beat R6s (frozen-random J co-trained identically) -- if R6s matches R6, the win came from g/contrastive plumbing, not learned synchrony; and dP5 must stay negative without instance-level leakage (verify via within-task-shuffle eval already in harness).
proposals.cheapest_validation_milestone: ~1-2 GPU-hours on shapes (10-way, 5 tasks, n_per_class~300, 1-2 seeds). GO/NO-GO single number: does R6 PhaseLock keep dP5 <= -0.25 AND final_acc >= 0.35 at the end of the 5-task stream (vs frozen-phase R6 baseline dP5~-0.01, final~0.13)? Concretely: (1) implement the differentiable Torch _phase_context (the only nontrivial code change; unit-test it matches the numpy group_directions+_pool_phase_state to 1e-4 on a random tensor, and that grad flows to a dummy J). (2) Add L_supcon + L_lock + prototype EMA + L_proto to a copy of run_cl_reg, unfreeze AKOrN inside the loop. (3) Run R6 and R6s, 1 seed each, plus re-confirm the oracle ~0.52 in the same code path as a liveness check. NO-GO if dP5 stays >-0.1 (phase never separates) or learn_acc craters <0.30 (separability kills discriminability) -- either kills the bet cheaply before scaling to CIFAR/multi-seed.

proposals.name: PALM: Phase-Attractor Library with Meta-settling (co-trained oscillator + hypernet, growth-protected coupling)
proposals.core_idea: Co-train AKOrN's coupling and the hypernet so that for each task the oscillator dynamics RAPIDLY SETTLE any unlabeled input into a dedicated, pre-allocated attractor basin in tangent-space phase, and the hypernet g reads that settled phase to emit head weights. Two changes vs the current capture-then-freeze harness: (1) The coupling is decomposed J_eff(t) = J_shared + diag(s_t) where J_shared (the KLayer.connectivity conv) is meta-trained on task 0 then FROZEN, and only a tiny per-task gain/bias vector s_t (a few hundred scalars modulating the omega natural-frequencies and the connectivity output channels) is learned per task -- so a new task is settled into a FRESH region by carving a new basin, never by rewriting the shared coupling. (2) A meta/settling loss makes the 2n-dim pooled phase context converge within T inner Kuramoto steps to a task-specific phase anchor mu_t that is allocated FAR from all previous anchors, giving an explicit, label-free, separable context the hypernet conditions on.
proposals.what_is_trained_vs_frozen: FROZEN throughout (C1): the random conv trunk (trunk in _build_pieces, already requires_grad_(False)); the AKOrN feature path that produces oscillator states EXCEPT the two small modulators below; and after task 0, J_shared = every KLayer.connectivity conv weight (the relational coupling) and the base omega. TRAINED on task 0 only, then frozen: J_shared and base omega via a short meta/settling phase (replaces the current ctx_epochs classification warmup). TRAINED on every task t: (a) a per-task gain vector s_t -- one scalar per oscillator channel group scaling KLayer.connectivity's output plus a per-group additive omega offset, total ~2*ch ≈ 128 params/task, attached as a hook on layer[2]; (b) the hypernet g = PhaseContextThetaGen.parameters() (the existing opt_g). So the task-adaptive trainable set per task = {s_t (~128), g}. The substrate coupling J_shared and omega never change after task 0 -> structurally satisfies C3. R6s control: identical pipeline but J_shared is the frozen-RANDOM connectivity (build_R6_scrambled), only s_t and g train -- so any win must come from the LEARNED meta-settled coupling (C4).
proposals.losses: Per training step on task t (operating on settled phase, i.e. run the KLayer T inner steps with J_eff=J_shared+diag(s_t)): (1) Classification CE: crit(hh.logits(ctx_settled, f), y) -- the existing loss. (2) Settling/anchor loss L_set = || pool_phase(state_T) - mu_t ||^2 averaged over the batch, pulling every task-t input's settled context to anchor mu_t (mu_t is a fixed unit-norm vector chosen at the task boundary by farthest-point sampling from {mu_0..mu_{t-1}} on the 2n-sphere; not learned). (3) Separation/repel hinge L_sep = mean_max(0, margin - ||pool_phase(state_T) - mu_j||^2) over previous anchors j<t, so the new basin sits clear of old ones. (4) Rapid-settling regularizer L_rapid = || pool_phase(state_k) - mu_t ||^2 summed over inner steps k=1..T with increasing weight (meta-objective: converge in few steps). On task 0 ONLY, J_shared+omega also receive grads from L_set+L_sep+CE (the meta-train of the substrate). On tasks>0, J_shared/omega are frozen and only s_t, g get grads. Total = CE + a*L_set + b*L_sep + c*L_rapid (a≈1, b≈0.5, c≈0.3). Plus the existing latent context-replay term on a tiny buffer of past (settled-ctx, feat, label) for g only.
proposals.cl_protocol: Class-incremental, 5 tasks (shapes) or SplitCIFAR-10, exactly the run_cl_reg loop structure. Task 0 boundary: meta-train J_shared+omega+s_0+g with full loss for ctx_epochs, allocate mu_0, then FREEZE J_shared and omega. Every task t>0 boundary: (i) allocate mu_t by farthest-point sampling on the unit sphere away from {mu_j: j<t} (label-free, deterministic from t); (ii) instantiate fresh s_t initialized to 0 (so J_eff starts = J_shared, a sane prior basin) and register it; (iii) train {s_t, g} for `epochs` with CE+L_set+L_sep+L_rapid + replay, J_shared frozen; (iv) push k=n_anchors/T settled-context exemplars (ctx, feat, label) into the replay buffer for g. At each boundary record the full TxT accuracy matrix (existing A). Store the small library {mu_t, s_t} (5 tasks * ~128 = ~640 scalars + 5 anchors) -- this is the grown, protected phase-attractor library.
proposals.how_it_stays_label_free_at_test: At test there is NO task id (C2). An input x is pushed through the AKOrN with EACH library gain s_t in turn (5 cheap inner-step rollouts, vectorized over the small s_t set) producing 5 candidate settled contexts; the system picks the rollout whose settled phase lands closest to its own anchor, i.e. t* = argmin_t || pool_phase(settle(x; s_t)) - mu_t ||. This is pure dynamics+geometry, no labels. The won basin's settled context c* = pool_phase(settle(x; s_{t*})) is fed to g -> head. The mechanism is self-organizing by construction: meta-settling trained each task's inputs to fall into that task's basin only under that task's gain, so the correct gain yields the tightest convergence (lowest residual to its anchor). Sanity already in harness: wrong-context dP5 must be strongly negative (feeding another task's settled context degrades acc) confirming the channel, not labels, carries task. Fallback if argmin is too brittle: soft-select (softmax over -residuals) and feed the residual-weighted mixture context to g -- still label-free, differentiable, and degrades gracefully.
proposals.anti_forgetting_mechanism: C3 is addressed at the substrate level, not by regularization hope. The oscillator's task-phase regions cannot be overwritten because the only shared dynamical parameters (J_shared conv weights + base omega) are FROZEN after task 0; each later task gets its OWN additive gain s_t that is never touched again once its task ends. Settling input x under gain s_a always reproduces task a's basin around mu_a regardless of how many later tasks were learned -- this is parameter isolation in the dynamics (like a hypernet-indexed mask, but on Kuramoto coupling), so forgetting of the PHASE itself is structurally ~0. The anchors mu_t are fixed targets allocated by farthest-point sampling, so basins are provably separated and a new basin is carved in empty phase space (L_sep enforces the margin), never on top of an old one. For g's own forgetting (it is shared across tasks), three guards: (i) g only ever sees well-separated, anchor-snapped contexts (the input distribution to g per task is a tight cluster near mu_t, minimizing interference); (ii) the existing latent context-replay buffer of settled exemplars; (iii) optionally freeze/grow g's last layer per anchor region. Critically, even if g drifts, the phase library and its label-free routing are intact, so re-deriving the head is cheap. This is exactly what the oracle (one-hot id) gets for free; PALM manufactures the one-hot equivalent from frozen dynamics.
proposals.why_it_closes_the_gap: The 0.24 ceiling came from a phase channel optimized ONLY for classification then frozen -- its task-separability was incidental, so g had low-capacity, overlapping contexts (C_ctx << H(T)). PALM directly maximizes C_ctx: L_set+L_sep push the 5 task contexts to mutually-far fixed anchors on the sphere, so the context distribution becomes ~5 tight, linearly-separated clusters -- approaching the one-hot oracle's geometry, which retains ~0.52. Because the contexts are nearly one-hot-separable AND the dynamics deterministically map each task's inputs to its anchor under the right gain, g's job collapses to the oracle's job (map a near-one-hot context to a head), so task-acc should move from 0.24 toward the 0.52 oracle ceiling. The per-task gain s_t is the extra capacity that lets a frozen shared coupling still carve fresh separable basins, which capture-then-freeze lacked. Co-training (CE backprops through the inner Kuramoto settle into s_t and into g) is what aligns the settled phase geometry with what g can actually decode -- the missing ingredient the post-hoc supervised-contrastive readout could not supply because it left the phase fixed.
proposals.main_failure_mode: Label-free routing collapse at test: if basins overlap or settling is not gain-selective enough, the argmin-over-gains t* picks the wrong basin, and routing accuracy (not within-basin accuracy) becomes the bottleneck -- you would see high oracle-routed acc but low self-routed acc, i.e. the channel works only when told which gain to use. This is the C2 failure and the thing to watch first. Secondary risk: L_sep/L_set make phase trivially encode task in a way that ignores the input (degenerate basin where settling is gain-driven, not input-driven) -- a C4 cheat caught by the within-task-shuffle eval (context from a different task-t input must still help) and by checking that routing residuals actually separate true-task inputs from off-task inputs. Mitigation for both: tune the inner-step T and the L_rapid weight so basins are reached only by genuinely on-task inputs, and use soft-routing if hard argmin is brittle.
proposals.cheapest_validation_milestone: On the 5-task shapes construct (CPU/1 GPU-hr, n_per_class small, 2-3 seeds): run task 0 meta-settling + allocate mu_0..mu_4 by farthest-point sampling, then the full CL loop with frozen J_shared and per-task s_t. GO/NO-GO in two cheap numbers reusing existing metrics: (1) ROUTING: label-free argmin-over-gains test-time task selection accuracy must beat 1/5 chance and approach the within-task-shuffle ceiling -- this is the make-or-break for C2 and can be measured after just task 1 vs task 0 (binary routing). (2) SEPARATION + ANTI-FORGET: linear_task_decodability on the settled (anchor-snapped) contexts must jump well above the ~0.24-era value toward oracle-like separability, AND after training all 5 tasks, re-settling task-0 inputs under s_0 must still land within margin of mu_0 (phase-region retention ~ unchanged, the C3 check) -- compare R6 (meta-learned J_shared) vs R6s (frozen-random J_shared) co-trained identically; R6 routing+separation must exceed R6s (C4). If routing acc collapses to chance or R6 does not beat R6s, NO-GO before any SplitCIFAR-10 spend.

proposals.name: PhaseSlot-AKOrN: Allocation-Protected Co-Trained Oscillator Slots (PSA)
proposals.core_idea: Co-train the AKOrN coupling J and the hypernet g, but partition the L1 oscillator into K disjoint "phase slots" (each slot = a contiguous block of oscillator channel-groups; widen L1 so each of 5 tasks owns its own block, e.g. expand from 64ch/4 groups to ~160ch/20 groups, ~4 groups per task). Each task t recruits ONE fresh slot s(t): during task t we train ONLY the coupling weights that read into and write within slot s(t)'s channels (a masked sub-conv of the connectivity Conv2d) plus the hypernet g, leaving all previously-allocated slots' coupling columns FROZEN. The pooled context c becomes the concatenation of all slots' 2n-group descriptors; g maps c->theta(head). At test, a frozen-random input-driven router computes a per-sample gate over slots from the trunk feature, zeroing the context contribution of inactive slots so phase self-organizes to the right context with no task id. This is the seed's "protected phase slot" idea, but made concrete by (a) EXPANDING the oscillator (n=4 is too small to carry H(T) for 5 tasks; the M2 modesty is a capacity-starvation symptom), (b) protecting slots via column-frozen coupling rather than soft regularization, and (c) a learned-but-frozen router for label-free recall.
proposals.what_is_trained_vs_frozen: TRAINED (per task t): (1) the coupling sub-matrix J[:, slot_s(t)] and J[slot_s(t), slot_s(t)] -- the connectivity Conv2d weights whose OUTPUT channels lie in slot s(t) (input channels may read globally so the slot can sense the input, but writes are slot-local); (2) the GroupNorm affine params for slot s(t)'s groups; (3) the hypernet g (PhaseContextThetaGen) -- but with per-slot output masking so g's task-t update predominantly uses the c-coordinates of slot s(t) (a learned input gate on g.fc1 restricted to active+already-frozen slots). FROZEN ALWAYS: the frozen-random task-agnostic trunk (C1 unbypassability -- never touched); the omega/readout-projection scaffolding shared across slots is frozen-random after init (param/geometry-matched between R6 and R6s). FROZEN AFTER ALLOCATION: every slot s(t') for t'<t -- its coupling columns and GroupNorm affines are hard-frozen (requires_grad_=False) the moment task t' ends. R6s control = identical pipeline but the trainable coupling sub-matrices are frozen-random (never updated), isolating whether LEARNED slot-local coupling is what carries the channel.
proposals.losses: Per task t, minibatch loss = CE(g(c)->head applied to trunk feats, y_t)  [main task loss, only on current task data]  + lambda_orth * sum over frozen slots s' of <pooled c_active, pooled c_s'>^2  [slot-orthogonality: pushes the NEW slot's phase descriptor to be linearly separable from all frozen slots' descriptors, so contexts don't collide -- this is the term that buys C_ctx>=H(T)]  + lambda_route * CE(router_gate, slot_onehot(t))  [router head: the frozen-random router's small TRAINABLE linear gate learns to map trunk feats to the current slot id using the task label available during training (C2 allows current-task labels at TRAIN time)]  + lambda_sparse * ||gate||_1 over slots  [encourages one-hot-ish slot activation so recall is crisp]. lambda_orth ramped 0->0.3 over task; lambda_route=1.0; lambda_sparse=1e-3. No replay buffer required (allocation handles forgetting), but a tiny 16-sample/slot latent buffer of (c_frozen, feats, y) can be kept purely to re-fit the router gate (router is a single linear layer, cheap to keep calibrated) -- this does NOT touch coupling or slots.
proposals.cl_protocol: Class-incremental, 5 tasks (shapes binding 10-way, 2 classes/task; or SplitCIFAR-10). At task boundary t->t+1: (1) freeze slot s(t): J columns writing into s(t)'s channels and s(t)'s GroupNorm affines get requires_grad_(False), recorded in a frozen-slot registry; (2) allocate the next slot s(t+1) from the pre-reserved expanded channel pool, re-enable grads only for its coupling columns; (3) snapshot a 16-sample latent calibration set (c, feats, y) for the router; (4) reset the g optimizer's momentum on the newly-active c-coordinates. Within a task: alternate ~the existing 40 epochs training g + active-slot coupling jointly. Eval after each task fills the T x T forgetting matrix exactly as run_cl_reg does (final_acc, forgetting, wrong_ctx_dP5). Slot count K is pre-sized to n_tasks (allocation must not exceed reserved width; if tasks > slots, fall back to least-recently-used slot sharing -- out of scope for the 5-task testbed).
proposals.how_it_stays_label_free_at_test: At test there is NO task id. The pooled context c is built from ALL slots' phase descriptors (concatenated 2n per slot). The frozen-random router (a fixed random projection of the trunk feature followed by the small trained linear gate) produces a per-sample soft gate over slots from the INPUT ALONE; this gate multiplies each slot's block of c before c enters g. Because (a) each slot's coupling was trained to fire its phase pattern only for its task's input statistics, and (b) the orthogonality loss made slots' descriptors separable, an input from task A drives slot-A oscillators into their learned phase attractor while other slots stay near their frozen-random baseline; the router gate then up-weights slot A. So context self-organizes to task A with zero label. Crucially the router only needs to identify WHICH slot, not the class -- a much easier, lower-entropy decision, and it is the same input-driven selection the seed proposed, made explicit. The oracle (one-hot true slot) remains as the C3-liveness ceiling (~0.52); PSA's gap to oracle measures router error.
proposals.anti_forgetting_mechanism: This is the crux (C3). Forgetting of task A's phase region happens ONLY if learning task B mutates the dynamics that produce slot-A's phase. PSA makes that structurally impossible at the COUPLING level, not via soft penalty: when task A finishes, every coupling weight whose OUTPUT lands in slot-A channels is hard-frozen (requires_grad_(False)) and slot-A's GroupNorm affines are frozen. Task B trains ONLY slot-B's output columns. Therefore the map (input -> slot-A oscillator phase) is a fixed function for all t>=A -- slot-A's attractor cannot drift. Two residual leak paths and their plugs: (i) cross-slot READ -- slot-B coupling may READ slot-A channels as input; we forbid writes into A but allow reads, and slot-A's VALUES are computed by frozen weights, so reading them cannot change A's phase. The only thing B can do is let A's phase influence B, which is harmless (it never corrupts A's own descriptor). (ii) shared GroupNorm running stats across slots -- we use per-slot GroupNorm groups (already n-grouped) so A's normalization statistics are computed within A's groups and are frozen with A. (iii) g forgetting (the von Oswald hazard) -- g's input gate is masked so a task-B update flows mainly through slot-B c-coordinates; combined with frozen-slot orthogonality, g's response to slot-A contexts is largely protected, and the 16-sample router calibration set lets us cheaply detect/repair any g-side drift. Net: slot allocation + column-freeze gives provable zero drift of each task's phase attractor; the only forgetting that can remain is router mis-routing, which is bounded and measurable against oracle.
proposals.why_it_closes_the_gap: M2's 0.24 task-only acc is capacity-starved: n=4 group-axes -> 8-dim context shared by all 5 tasks, so contexts collide and g cannot separate tasks (joint lift collapses to ~0.12-0.44 with high variance, exactly a too-small-C_ctx signature). PSA attacks both terms of C_ctx >= H(T): it ENLARGES the channel (5 disjoint slots, each carrying its own 2n descriptor, so C_ctx scales with K) AND it CO-TRAINS the coupling so each slot's phase is actively SHAPED to be task-discriminative (the post-hoc readout null proved frozen phase can't be amplified -- the phase itself must be shaped, which co-training does). The orthogonality loss directly maximizes inter-slot separability = directly raises I(c;theta). Because the oracle (clean per-task context) already reaches ~0.52 in this exact harness with ~0.02 forgetting, providing g with near-clean, non-colliding, drift-free per-slot contexts should track the oracle: PSA's contexts ARE per-task and frozen-stable by construction, so the achievable ceiling is the oracle minus router error, plausibly 0.40-0.50 vs the 0.24 floor.
proposals.main_failure_mode: Router collapse / slot non-separation: if the frozen-random trunk feature does not linearly separate the 5 tasks well enough, the trained router gate mis-routes at test (activates the wrong slot), and accuracy regresses toward chance even though each slot's phase is perfectly preserved -- i.e., we'd have solved forgetting but not label-free recall, converting a forgetting failure into a routing failure. A secondary mode: the slot-orthogonality loss fights the task loss when the expanded oscillator's input-reading coupling is too constrained, capping learn_acc. Diagnostic: compare PSA final_acc against an "oracle-router" variant (gate = true slot one-hot) -- if oracle-router approaches 0.52 but PSA lags, the bottleneck is the router, not the slot mechanism; if both lag, slots aren't carrying enough.
proposals.cheapest_validation_milestone: On the 5-task shapes binding construct, n=2-3 seeds, CPU/1-GPU-hour: run PSA vs three controls in the existing run_cl_reg harness extended with slot masking -- (1) PSA-R6 (learned slot coupling), (2) PSA-R6s (frozen-random slot coupling, C4 control), (3) oracle-router PSA (gate=true slot). GO if PSA-R6 final_acc clears ~0.40 with forgetting < ~0.10 AND beats PSA-R6s by a clear margin AND wrong_ctx_dP5 stays strongly negative (channel real, no bypass). The single cheapest discriminating number: PSA-R6 final_acc vs the existing R6 naive-CL 0.133 baseline and the 0.52 oracle ceiling -- if it lands above ~0.40 the allocation+expansion bet is validated; if it sits near 0.133 the slots aren't protecting/carrying and it's NO-GO. Implementation cost is small: add a channel-slot mask + per-slot freeze registry to _build_pieces, a 1-line masked-coupling step, the orthogonality + router loss terms, and a router gate multiply before g -- all localized to m2_hypernet.py.

critiques.proposal_name: PALM: Phase-Attractor Ladder Memory (grown Kuramoto bias-anchors + energy-based label-free attractor selection, co-trained with hypernet)
critiques.fatal_flaws:
PALM has one clever, genuinely-new idea (parameter-disjoint grown anchors over a frozen substrate, which DOES kill sequential forgetting structurally) bolted onto a label-free selector that the existing evidence says cannot work. Concretely:

(1) THE SELECTOR RESTS ON A QUANTITY ALREADY SHOWN TO BE NULL. PALM's whole label-free story is "argmin_j E_kur(x;b_j)". I read the real code: the "Kuramoto energy" is `sim = x*y` (klayer.py project()), an element-wise alignment between the update and the unit-sphere-normalized state. The selector therefore lives in exactly the synchrony/phase scalar that this project already measured to death. Three independent results in /Users/harry/Desktop/temp/test/NeuralCombs/experiments/m2/AUTONOMOUS-PLAN-2026-06-07.md kill it: (a) task-ID is NOT cleanly decodable from R6 phase, linear NULL at n=17 (Holm>0.3 all L1 descriptors) AND nonlinear MLP null, "probe-fragile"; (b) within-task-shuffle task-level acc is only ~0.24 vs 0.10 chance vs 0.52 oracle; (c) and the decisive one — "R6 vs R5:depthwise IS a small positive ... LEARNED vs RANDOM coupling does NOT" help decodability. The energy-argmin needs the energy surface to be task-separable from the input alone; the project's own n=17 verdict is that the synchrony channel does not carry a cleanly-separable task signal, and crucially that LEARNED coupling is no better than random at it. PALM bets the farm on extracting a clean task label from precisely the scalar that was demonstrated not to contain one.

(2) THE ANCHOR INJECTION POINT IS PARTIALLY NEUTRALIZED BY c_norm. PALM injects additive bias b_t "at the existing c = self.c_norm(c) injection point". I checked: c_norm is GroupNorm(affine=True) (ladder.py:93, confirmed in anatomy.md and the klayer dump). GroupNorm zero-means and unit-variance-normalizes per group BEFORE the bias enters dynamics. A constant additive DC field b_t broadcast over H,W is exactly the component GroupNorm removes/rescales. The affine gamma can partially restore a per-channel scale but the per-task DC offset that is supposed to "shift the basin" gets substantially attenuated. The proposal never reckons with the fact that its anchor is injected on the far side of a normalization layer designed to delete additive offsets.

(3) CO-TRAINING-J-ON-TASK-1-THEN-FREEZE IS A SEVERE ASYMMETRY. The shared landscape (J) is sculpted using ONLY task-1 data. Tasks 2..5 get only an additive anchor on top of a substrate that was never optimized to make THEIR inputs synchronize into a deep, distinct basin. The "attractor formation loss" L_attr for t>=2 has to carve a new deep basin using a few-hundred-param bias on a frozen landscape — far weaker than the claim that each task gets "a dedicated learned attractor that forces task-distinct fixed points". Task 1 will get a real basin; later tasks get shallow dents, so the energy gaps the selector needs will be systematically smaller for exactly the tasks added later.
critiques.will_it_forget_C3:
PARTIALLY YES — this is the proposal's one real strength, and the reviewer should not pretend otherwise. Mechanism (1) parameter-disjoint frozen anchors/heads is a genuine structural guarantee: requires_grad_(False) on old b_j/g_j means task-B's optimizer literally cannot move task-A's parameters. Mechanism (2) freezing J after task 1 removes the substrate drift that causes co-training's deep hazard (C3). So the CLASSIC oscillator self-forgetting (B re-sculpts A's phase region by moving J) IS blocked. This is the only part of PALM that addresses the stated crux honestly.

BUT C3 is not fully closed, it is RELOCATED into the selector. The oracle control already shows that with a PERFECT context the harness retains ~0.52 at ~0.02 forgetting. PALM's per-task heads, once frozen, will likewise retain their learn-acc. So "forgetting" measured the conventional way (head drift) goes to ~0. The catch: the EFFECTIVE forgetting is now selector mis-routing. At test, an old task-A input that the energy-argmin routes to a NEWER anchor b_j (j>A) is misclassified — and because later basins are shallower (asymmetry flaw 3) and the energy gaps small (flaw 1), this routing error grows with the number of tasks. So PALM converts "head forgetting" into "selector forgetting" and then reports the former as ~0. That is the subtle trap: it CAN truthfully claim near-zero forgetting on the frozen-head metric while final_acc still collapses, because final_acc = selector_acc x head_acc and the selector is the unprotected component. L_stab only pins the energy of old anchors on their OWN stored exemplars; it does nothing to guarantee that a NEW anchor doesn't become a lower-energy basin for OLD inputs (the cross-basin direction that actually causes mis-routing). The anti-forgetting machinery protects the heads it doesn't need to protect and leaves the selector — the real failure surface — only weakly defended by L_sep on 200 replay images.
critiques.will_it_stay_label_free_C2: Label-free AT TEST: borderline-OK as designed, but with a real leakage risk. The argmin-over-anchors selector uses only inputs at inference, so formally no task id is consumed at test — consistent with the project's accepted "no labels at inference" reading (class-IL training labels are fine). HOWEVER: L_attr, L_sep, L_stab are all driven by task-supervised data during training (which task's anchor to deepen, which past anchor to push above). That is legitimate class-IL but it means the selector's separability, IF it ever appears, is purchased with training-time task supervision — exactly the move the project flagged ("weakens claim from 'no task labels' to 'no task labels at inference'"). The deeper C2 worry is that the whole reason PALM might show separation is that b_t is being trained, per-task, to make task-t inputs settle low under b_t — i.e. the anchor learns to be a task detector. If the selector works, you must then prove it works because phase DYNAMICS separate tasks and not because b_t (a trained per-task parameter directly supervised on task membership) has become a thinly-disguised stored task prototype. The within-task-shuffle and constant-context dP5 invariants in the harness test head-bypass, NOT selector-as-memorized-prototype. There is no current invariant that distinguishes "energy basin self-organized from dynamics" from "b_t memorized a per-task template and the energy is just template-matching distance". That gap is a C2/C4 hole.
critiques.bypass_or_cheat_risk_C1_C4:
SEVERAL real risks.

C1 (unbypassability): SAFE-ish. The trunk stays frozen-random and the head theta still flows from g_t(c_phase). Good. One leak: the proposal adds a "tiny shared anchor-gate" that trains every task — anything shared-and-trainable is a static task-adaptive path that could partially bypass the phase channel; it must be audited (the project's whole M2 framing depends on the phase being the ONLY adaptive path). Keep the anchor-gate frozen or it violates C1.

C4 (cheating / beat-R6s-for-wrong-reason): HIGH. The decisive prior result is "LEARNED vs RANDOM coupling does NOT" improve task-decodability (n=17). PALM's claim "beats R6s because co-trained J forms deeper distinct basins" runs directly against that. More dangerous: PALM can beat R6s for a CHEATING reason — the per-task anchors b_t plus per-task heads g_t are a growing, parameter-disjoint bank that approximates a task-indexed memory. With T anchors and T heads, even a RANDOM J (R6s) can get decent selector accuracy if the anchors simply act as T learned linear template-matchers on the input-driven state. In that case PALM's gains come from the GROW-AND-FREEZE capacity bank (a progressive-net trick that has nothing to do with synchrony) rather than from learned Kuramoto binding. The required control is brutal and the proposal half-acknowledges it: run the ENTIRE PALM pipeline with R6s (frozen-random J) co-trained identically. If PALM-on-R6s also retains 0.40+, the synchrony/co-training thesis is FALSIFIED and PALM is just expandable networks in disguise. Given the n=17 "learned=random" result, I expect PALM-on-R6s to be uncomfortably close to PALM-on-R6.

INSTANCE-LEAK cheat: the n=20 decomposition showed ~54% of the joint lift is INSTANCE-level. The energy selector could route by instance similarity (which stored exemplars' basin this image is nearest), not task — passing selector-accuracy on the test split while really doing nearest-exemplar retrieval over the 200-image buffer. Selector accuracy must be reported on inputs with NO exemplar of their class in the buffer, or it is contaminated by instance leak.
critiques.bug_risk:
Where subtle bugs will hide:
- ENERGY SIGN/POOLING: `sim = x*y` is per-element and `argmin` direction depends on whether high sim = synchronized (good basin) or not, and on how it is pooled (mean over channels/space). Off-by-sign or wrong-axis pooling silently makes the selector anti-correlated with the right basin while L_sep happily trains a margin in the wrong direction. The proposal calls it "negative tangent-space synchrony" then also "lowest-energy basin = best synchronized" — those two framings can flip the sign.
- DETERMINISM: x is re-randomized every forward (`x = torch.randn_like(c)`, knet.py:182) and the harness wraps phase capture in h3_seeded(0) for a reason. If the per-anchor energies at test are computed under DIFFERENT random inits, the argmin is comparing apples to noise. Every candidate-anchor forward must share the SAME init seed or selector accuracy is corrupted.
- c_norm INTERACTION (above): the anchor enters before GroupNorm; gradient to b_t flows through the normalization, so its effective learning is coupled to the affine params, easy to get a b_t that "trains" (loss drops) while contributing ~nothing post-norm.
- E_kur*(j) SNAPSHOT DRIFT: L_stab targets a stored scalar taken right after task j, but that scalar was computed at a particular init/replay sample; re-evaluating on the (reservoir-updated, capped-200) buffer later compares against a stale target on a shifted sample set.
- L_sep over "a few frozen past anchors": with reservoir cap 200 over 5 tasks, late tasks have very few per-task exemplars to enforce margins, so the margin loss is estimated on a handful of images — high-variance, easy to overfit the buffer and not generalize the selector.
critiques.salvage_or_fix:
RUN THE GO/NO-GO BACKWARDS AND HARSHER. The proposal's own 2-task cheapest-validation milestone is the right instinct but the GO bar is wrong — it must include the falsifying control. Single most valuable change: in the 2-task slice, ALSO run the identical PALM pipeline on R6s (frozen-random J) and on a deliberately-trivial "anchor = stored task-centroid, energy = L2 distance to centroid" baseline. Decision rule: PALM-on-R6 is only worth pursuing if its SELECTOR accuracy beats BOTH (a) R6s-PALM and (b) the centroid-distance baseline by a clear, paired margin — because the prior n=17 result ("learned coupling not better than random") predicts it will NOT, and the grow-and-freeze bank predicts the centroid baseline will match it. If PALM only beats naive sequential but ties R6s/centroid, it has proven progressive-networks works, not that synchrony does — which is NOT the thesis.

Secondary fixes if it survives that gate: (1) move the anchor injection to ADD AFTER c_norm (or make b_t a per-group affine delta) so normalization doesn't eat it; (2) protect the selector directly — add a cross-basin margin term that pushes NEW anchors' energy on OLD inputs ABOVE old anchors' energy (the actual mis-routing direction), not just the within-task L_stab; (3) report selector accuracy ONLY on held-out classes with zero exemplars in the replay buffer (kills the instance-leak cheat); (4) freeze the shared anchor-gate (C1). If after all that the selector still clears chance and beats R6s+centroid, then — and only then — the energy-argmin-over-grown-anchors idea is a real contribution.

critiques.proposal_name: PALACE: Phase-Anchored Low-rank Coupling with EMA consolidation
critiques.fatal_flaws:
PALACE's own "cheapest validation milestone" (label-free router slot-accuracy from the frozen-J0 order-parameter descriptor) has ALREADY been run in this repo and FAILED, before PALACE was written. The whole design rides on one assumption: that the frozen-J0 first-pass phase descriptor is task-discriminative enough for a label-free router to pick the right slot. The artifacts say it is not.

EVIDENCE 1 — the router signal is dead AND not even synchrony-specific (C4 violation built in). experiments/m2/results/autorun_2026_06_07/step4a_readout.json (n=5/arm): MLP task-decode of L1 phase = R6 0.294 vs R6s 0.297 (IDENTICAL); linear cv R6 0.254 vs R6s 0.251; only +0.078 above the within-task shuffle floor (0.216). The router reads exactly this descriptor. PALACE's GO gate demands slot-acc >= 0.45; the measured ceiling is ~0.29 and the frozen-RANDOM control decodes equally well — so even if the router hit 0.45 it would do so on R6s too, failing C4. The plan file states the conclusion outright: "task-ID is NOT cleanly DECODABLE from phase (R6~R6s, linear n=17 null + nonlinear null, probe-fragile)." PALACE's own stated main_failure_mode (router collapse) is therefore not a risk — it is a measured fact.

EVIDENCE 2 — co-trained phase-shaping (PALACE's L2 hinge / symmetry-breaker) is a CONFIRMED NULL. step4b_lam0 vs step4b_lam1 (n=8): a supervised-contrastive separation objective on the LIVE co-trained phase (precisely PALACE's "shape the live phase, not frozen phase" pitch) moved task_lift 0.124 -> 0.121 (delta -0.003, p=0.84) and task_decode_cv 0.430 -> 0.432 (p=0.91). The plan's verdict: "the phase task-channel ceiling looks ARCHITECTURAL, not training-effort." PALACE re-proposes the same family of mechanism (push centroids apart) and asserts it will work because it is on live phase — but the live-phase version was already tried and is flat.

So both load-bearing legs (label-free selection AND live-phase separation) are independently falsified in artifacts dated the same day as the GOAL handoff. PALACE is a more elaborate wrapper around two already-dead mechanisms.
critiques.will_it_forget_C3: The C3 story is the strongest-engineered part and the part that DOESN'T matter, because C3 is downstream of selection. Structural locality (slot-local dJ_t, b_t) genuinely prevents task B's gradients from overwriting task A's KNOB tensors — that claim is true and is a real advance over a slow global J. SI/EWC + EMA freeze + replay on g/router are standard and plausible. BUT: anti-forgetting protects a channel that is only usable if the right knob is injected at test, and the right knob is selected by the router, and the router is provably at chance-vs-synchrony-control (Evidence 1). A perfectly non-overwritable knob that you cannot SELECT at test is worthless. Worse, the soft-mix sum_k w_k{dJ_k,b_k} under a smeared router produces an averaged knob -> phase lands between basins -> regress toward the ~0.13 naive collapse (the repo already shows naive CL final_acc=0.131 in step4c_base, vs oracle 0.524). The oracle's 0.02 forgetting that PALACE cites as proof "g doesn't forget given the right knob" is exactly the point: the oracle is a ONE-HOT TRUE TASK ID. PALACE's router is the thing that must reconstruct that one-hot label-free, and it cannot. So C3 is solved for the wrong object; sequential forgetting of the KNOB is prevented but sequential degradation of ROUTING is the actual failure and is not addressed by any of the three levels.
critiques.will_it_stay_label_free_C2: At test, formally label-free: only the router's TRAINING target uses task id (legitimate class-IL), inference uses the order-parameter descriptor. The mechanism is C2-clean in principle. The problem is not legality but EMPTINESS: the label-free descriptor carries near-zero synchrony-specific task bits (R6 MLP 0.294 == R6s 0.297, +0.078 over floor). The descriptor is rotation/permutation-invariant by design, which is correct for label-freeness, but the S_N symmetry the project repeatedly hit means the invariant descriptor washes out exactly the task structure the router needs. So C2 is satisfied trivially by a channel that conveys almost nothing — a Pyrrhic compliance.
critiques.bypass_or_cheat_risk_C1_C4: Two real cheat risks, both already visible in artifacts. (C4 R6s) The router's decode signal is identical for R6 and R6s (step4a), so any router "success" would be coupling-agnostic and fail the must-beat-R6s bar. (C4 instance leak) The repo's n=20 decomposition shows ~54% of the joint context lift is INSTANCE-level, not task-level (real 0.327, within-shuffle 0.141 task vs 0.186 instance). PALACE's L_route trains the router on the CURRENT task's inputs; a 2-layer MLP on an instance-rich descriptor can learn to route on exemplar identity (the 54% instance component), inflating train slot-acc while failing held-out within-task-shuffle and wrong-context dP5 — the exact secondary risk PALACE itself names but does not defend against (its replay buffer is only 8 exemplars/task, plenty to memorize, not enough to generalize routing). On CIFAR (step5) seed 0 shows task_lift 0.008 with instance_lift 0.043 — almost pure instance leak. C1 (unbypassability) is preserved structurally since the trunk stays frozen-random; that constraint is fine. The danger is C4, and it is live.
critiques.bug_risk: (1) Teacher-forcing mismatch: training forces w to true one-hot for clean knob gradients, but test uses soft router weights. The slow knobs dJ_t,b_t are thus only ever trained at the one-hot operating point; under a smeared test-time mixture they enter an untrained convex-combination regime (knob arithmetic is not linear in phase outcome since it passes through nonlinear Kuramoto dynamics + apply_proj). Train/test distribution shift on the knob is a silent accuracy killer not captured by any joint upper bound. (2) Injecting dJ_t = U_t V_t^T as an additive delta to the L1 connectivity OUTPUT (self.connectivity(x)+c in ladder.py LadderCore / the real KLayer) is a per-channel C x C linear map applied to the conv output; rank-4 over C=64 with frozen J0 may be too weak to move a fixed-point that J0 dominates, OR the kupdate normalization (c_norm GroupNorm, sphere_normalize) may absorb b_t entirely — b_t added pre-norm gets renormalized away. Either makes the knob a no-op. (3) The order-parameter descriptor (group-direction Gram eigenvalues + global Kuramoto R) is a NEW descriptor not the one step4a tested (step4a used the 136-dim pooled L1 phase); if PALACE claims its descriptor is more discriminative it must re-measure — but the project already found nonlinear probes null across descriptor families, so the prior is strongly against it. (4) EMA-freezing LR->0 plus SI penalty plus replay triple-counts protection and can over-constrain g, freezing it before later tasks learn.
critiques.salvage_or_fix: The single change that matters: do NOT build any of the consolidation/locality/replay machinery. Run ONLY the already-defined go/no-go FIRST and honestly — and recognize it is essentially already done. step4a (router decode) is at chance-vs-control and step4b (live-phase separation) is null at n=8. Before writing a single line of PALACE, the honest action is to STOP: the label-free selection signal demonstrably is not there (R6==R6s, +0.078 over floor), which by PALACE's own NO-GO criterion kills it ("router slot-acc ~ chance ... the sequential consolidation machinery would be protecting an unusable channel"). If one insists on a last swing, the only scientifically new lever is to attack the ARCHITECTURAL ceiling the project identified: replace the rotation/permutation-INVARIANT descriptor (which by construction destroys task structure on a single-object construct) with a genuine multi-object/spatial-binding construct where AKOrN's coupling has something to bind (the research log repeatedly flags construct-mismatch as the root cause: AKOrN binds spatial groups; the shapes task has one centered object). That is a construct redesign, not PALACE. PALACE as specified spends its entire budget protecting a channel the same repo has already measured to be unusable for label-free selection.

critiques.proposal_name: PhaseLock: co-trained phase-region locking with prototype-anchored contrastive separation and dual-replay
critiques.fatal_flaws:
Three flaws, the first two near-fatal as written, all verified against the repo (m2_hypernet.py, results/m2_hypernet_joint.json, anatomy.md, research-log.md).

(1) dP5 is NOT a clean falsifier and CANNOT separate R6 from R6s. The proposal repeatedly leans on "dP5 strongly negative = phase-driven, label-free routing" as the GO signal and the C4 proof. But the EXISTING joint upper-bound data already shows R6s (frozen-random coupling) gets dP5 = -0.13 to -0.32 (results/m2_hypernet_joint.json), and research-log.md line ~160 states it outright: "joint dP5 R6 -0.39 vs R6s -0.18." A frozen-random conv produces a pooled phase descriptor whose wrong-context swap also craters accuracy, because the descriptor co-varies with the trunk features per-image. So a "dP5 <= -0.25" GO gate is satisfiable by the R6s control. The headline falsifier measures "is the context input used at all," not "did LEARNED synchrony make it usable." The bet can pass its own GO/NO-GO for the wrong reason.

(2) The anti-forgetting mechanism fights physics that the repo already disproved. J is a SINGLE shared convolution (LadderCore/KLayer.connectivity), reused across ALL tasks and all T=3 recurrence steps; there is NO per-task parameter anywhere in the oscillator. L_lock is explicitly "EWC-style elastic anchor in phase space on J." But EWC-in-weight-space was already run and was NULL: research-log.md shows EWC sits at the 78-79pt no-replay forgetting ceiling, indistinguishable from naive. The user's own stated CCC bound is "regularization can't raise capacity, only re-feeding old data does." L_lock is regularization; moving it from weight space to phase space does not change which side of the impossibility bound it is on. The only force in PhaseLock that actually re-feeds data is L_replay/L_proto -- i.e., replay, which the logs already show "flatters R6" weakly and is fragile (m3_replay_R6: final 0.11-0.23, forgetting 0.47-0.69). So the genuinely load-bearing component is the one already shown to be weak; the three "novel" forces (supcon, lock, orthogonal frame) are the ones on the wrong side of the bound.

(3) The capacity arithmetic is incoherent with the actual channel. The "C_ctx >= H(T) = 2.3 bits" argument is applied to the wrong object. C_ctx in this harness is I(context; GENERATED THETA), and theta is one shared ~650-param linear head (feat_dim 64 -> 10 classes) emitted by an 8->64->flat MLP. Making the 8-dim pooled c cluster into 5 orthogonal basins (the supcon goal) raises I(c;task), the REPRESENTATIONAL quantity the prereg explicitly says is NECESSARY-BUT-NOT-C_ctx (preregistration-M2.md line ~98). The oracle hits 0.52 by feeding a literal one-hot that the MLP trivially routes; there is no guarantee that 5 separable-but-curved phase basins route through the SAME tiny MLP to 5 disjoint heads. The proposal conflates "separable context" with "disjoint generated-parameter regions."
critiques.will_it_forget_C3: Partially, and the part that works is the part that is NOT novel. C3 is real and the proposal diagnoses it correctly: training the shared J on task B drags task-A inputs out of their basin. But examine each defense against the substrate. (a) L_lock anchors only the ~k buffered exemplars per past task; the proposal itself concedes "J can satisfy L_lock on anchors while still drifting non-anchor task-A inputs." Because c is a permutation-invariant POOLED summary over ~1e5 sites and J is convolutional/translation-equivariant, satisfying the lock on a few hundred anchors gives almost no control over the basin of unseen task-A images -- the lock has a generalization hole exactly where forgetting lives. (b) The orthogonal target frame E only has T=5 rows in a 2n=8-dim space; one-hot-padded rows are fine, but the supcon pull is toward POOLED means, and a single shared J cannot independently place 5 task clusters at 5 fixed directions while ALSO preserving within-task class structure -- moving toward E[B] necessarily perturbs the conv that places E[A]. (c) L_proto/L_replay only protect g's readout, not J's placement; if J drifts (and (a)/(b) don't stop it), g is being trained to map p_A (a STALE prototype) to A's head while live c(x_A) has moved -- so at TEST, x_A produces a drifted context that g maps to the wrong head. The mechanism that would actually stop forgetting is genuine replay of raw exemplars through the live J (re-feeding data, CCC-legal), and that is buried as one of five terms rather than being the load-bearing one. Net: it will forget less than naive, but the reduction will be carried by replay, and the "phase-region lock" will not demonstrably stop oscillator self-forgetting on held-out inputs.
critiques.will_it_stay_label_free_C2: At TEST, yes -- the inference path is genuinely label-free (c from input alone, no task id), identical to the existing harness minus the freeze. That guardrail is respected. The subtle risk is during TRAINING: the supcon loss uses task-id as the positive key and binds each task's mean to a PRE-RESERVED row E[t] indexed by task id. This is C2-legal (current-task labels/ids allowed at train time), but it means the phase is being explicitly TAUGHT a task code, so a strongly-negative dP5 no longer proves "self-organized" routing -- it proves "we trained a 5-way task classifier into J and read it out." That is fine for the CL goal but it weakens the SCIENTIFIC claim ("phase self-organizes into context"). The dP5 falsifier cannot distinguish "J learned synchrony-based context" from "J was supervised into a 5-cluster task tag," and the within-task-shuffle eval only rules out instance-level leakage, not task-tag memorization. So C2 holds operationally, but the headline interpretation ("label-free emergence") is overclaimed: it is label-free at test only because the task tag was supervised in at train time.
critiques.bypass_or_cheat_risk_C1_C4: C1 (unbypassability) is preserved correctly: trunk stays frozen-random, so phase->theta remains the only adaptive route -- good. C4 is the real exposure. (i) The must-beat-R6s control is undermined by flaw (1): R6s already gets negative dP5 and non-trivial joint lift (real-acc up to 0.37, lift up to 0.23 in the JSON), so co-training R6s identically with supcon could let R6s ALSO hit dP5 <= -0.25 and final >= 0.35 -- the supcon loss can shape even a random-coupling oscillator's downstream pooled descriptor into 5 clusters, because the supcon gradient flows through whatever J is there. If R6s matches R6 under PhaseLock, the win came from "supcon + replay plumbing," not learned synchrony -- and nothing in the proposal's GO/NO-GO checks the R6-minus-R6s GAP, only R6's absolute numbers. (ii) Label-leak-into-phase: supcon-to-E[t] is a soft task-label injection into c; combined with prototype replay (synthetic contexts p_s + jitter with stored labels), g can learn an essentially one-hot-like 5-way tag, asymptotically approaching the ORACLE by construction rather than by synchrony -- i.e., it can hit ~0.52 as a near-tautology of having supervised the task tag in, which is a bypass of the scientific question even while obeying C1/C2. The C4 firewall must be "R6 minus R6s, both under full PhaseLock, with a pre-registered gap margin," and that is not the stated GO metric.
critiques.bug_risk: Highest-risk code is the differentiable phase-context port, which is the ONLY nontrivial change and is load-bearing for every loss. Specific hazards verified against the source: (1) Determinism/grad conflict: knet.py initializes oscillator state as x = torch.randn_like(c) EVERY forward, made deterministic only by _seeded(0) (h3.py) which the CURRENT code runs inside torch.no_grad().
