A differentiable port must drop no_grad but KEEP _seeded so the random init is fixed -- if the init RNG is reseeded per-batch but not per-identical-x, c(x_A) is not stable batch-to-batch and L_lock anchors a moving target (silent). (2) The unit-test "matches numpy group_directions+_pool_phase_state to 1e-4" will pass on the POOLED descriptor while the gradient path is still wrong: group_directions subsamples max_sites=20000 via a numpy RNG (h3.py:512) -- a Torch port must replicate the SAME fixed-index subsample or the descriptor (and its grad) differ; an L2-normalize with eps over the n-axis can produce near-zero-norm sites whose gradient explodes (the numpy version clips to 1e-12 but is detached). (3) The tangent projection project(y,x)=y-(x.y)x plus per-step sphere renormalize over T=3 with a SHARED J is a small unrolled recurrence; backprop is fine but the renormalize Jacobian is ill-conditioned near the poles, and second-order interaction with supcon can NaN -- needs grad clipping. (4) Prototype EMA stored as a non-grad buffer but L_lock = ||c(x)-p||^2 computed through live J: if p is accidentally on the graph (not .detach()ed) the lock pulls the prototype toward c instead of c toward p -- the exact inversion that would silently NULL the anti-forgetting term while still logging a small loss.
critiques.salvage_or_fix: Re-anchor the entire bet on the R6-minus-R6s GAP, not R6's absolute dP5/final, and make raw-exemplar replay-through-live-J the primary (CCC-legal) anti-forgetting force while DEMOTING supcon/lock/orthogonal-frame to ablation arms. Concretely: (a) Change the GO/NO-GO to "R6 final_acc minus R6s final_acc >= a pre-registered margin (e.g. 0.10) AND R6 dP5 minus R6s dP5 strongly negative," both arms run under IDENTICAL PhaseLock -- this is the only test that attributes any win to learned synchrony rather than supcon plumbing (kills the flaw-1 / C4 confound). (b) Run a critical ablation: PhaseLock-minus-supcon-minus-orthogonal-frame (replay+lock only) vs full PhaseLock; if full does not beat replay-only by a clear margin, the novel forces add nothing and the result is "replay works, as expected." (c) To make L_lock actually bind C3, anchor on raw INPUTS re-pushed through live J (so the lock generalizes off-buffer) rather than only stored exemplars, and lock c(x_A) computed with the SAME _seeded init used at test. (d) Add an explicit oracle-distance check: measure I(generated-theta; task) not just I(c; task), to confirm the win is in the PARAMETER channel (true C_ctx) and not merely a more separable representation -- otherwise it repeats the M2 "better readout on frozen phase was null" trap one level up.

critiques.proposal_name: PALM: Phase-Attractor Library with Meta-settling (co-trained oscillator + hypernet, growth-protected coupling)
critiques.fatal_flaws:
PALM has a hidden architecture switch dressed up as "co-training," plus several concrete defects:

1. IT IS NOT ACTUALLY CO-TRAINING THE COUPLING (the whole point of the new bet). The thesis says M3's fix is to "train the oscillator dynamics (coupling J) AND the hypernet g END-TO-END." PALM freezes J_shared and omega after task 0 and only ever trains a ~128-scalar per-task gain s_t plus g. So the shared Kuramoto coupling is co-trained on exactly ONE task, then it is capture-then-freeze for tasks 1..4 — the very paradigm M2/M3 already established is too weak. The "co-training" that aligns phase geometry with what g can decode (the stated missing ingredient) happens only on task 0; for every later task the substrate is frozen and the basin is carved purely by a 128-dim diagonal gain. This is a per-task adapter on a frozen random-ish coupling, i.e. structurally close to R6s-plus-a-mask. The mechanism that the proposal claims closes 0.24->0.52 (CE backprop through the inner settle into J) is disabled for 80% of the stream.

2. THE LABEL-FREE ROUTER IS A SECOND, UNTRAINED CLASSIFIER THAT MUST ITSELF NOT FORGET — and it has no anti-forgetting mechanism at all. At test, t* = argmin_t ||settle(x; s_t) - mu_t||. This routing-by-residual is a 5-way task classifier built from the s_t library. Its accuracy was never trained for and is never replayed. Crucially the oracle's 0.524 comes from a PERFECT one-hot id; PALM must MANUFACTURE that id from the residual argmin. If routing is even 70% accurate, you feed g the wrong basin context on 30% of inputs and accuracy collapses toward chance regardless of how clean the basins are. The proposal's own "main_failure_mode" admits this is the bottleneck, then hand-waves it with "tune T and soft-routing." The joint logs already show the danger: R6 joint lift is wildly seed-dependent (0.41, 0.117, 0.335 across 3 seeds) — separability is unstable at the JOINT optimum with no sequential pressure, so a residual-argmin router built on it will be brittle.

3. ANCHOR GEOMETRY IS INFEASIBLE GIVEN THE ACTUAL POOLING. Verified in code: _pool_phase_state returns concatenate([mean_per_axis, meansq_per_axis]); with BACKBONE n=4 the context is 2n=8-dim, and the second 4 dims (meansq) are ALWAYS >= 0. Farthest-point sampling mu_t on the full unit 2n-sphere will place anchors with negative meansq coordinates that NO real settled context can ever reach. So L_set pulls contexts toward unreachable targets, the residual floor is nonzero and task-dependent, and the argmin router's margins are corrupted by this systematic offset. The "provably separated basins on the 2n-sphere" claim is false for this descriptor.

4. CAPACITY ARITHMETIC IS UNARGUED. ctx_dim is only 8 (2n=4*2). Per task the adaptive substrate freedom is ~128 scalars (s_t) feeding an 8-dim context. The claim that 5 tight clusters in 8-d approach the one-hot oracle geometry is plausible in principle, but the binding/decodability work already found task-id is NOT cleanly decodable from this 2n context even with nonlinear MLP probes (Morning report 2(c): R6~=R6s, null). PALM asserts L_set+L_sep will fix this, but on tasks>0 the only lever to move the context is s_t through a FROZEN coupling — there is no evidence a diagonal gain on frozen J can make a non-decodable channel decodable.
critiques.will_it_forget_C3:
PARTIALLY — it solves the easy half of C3 and ignores the hard half.

What it genuinely fixes: parameter-isolation of the DYNAMICS. Because J_shared+omega are frozen after task 0 and each task gets its own never-touched s_t, re-settling task-0 inputs under s_0 will reproduce roughly the same trajectory regardless of later tasks. So "phase-region forgetting" of a given basin is structurally near-zero. This is real and is the proposal's strongest claim. (Caveat: omega is global_omg/learn_omg in BACKBONE, and the proposal adds per-group omega OFFSETS inside s_t — fine, those are per-task too.)

What it does NOT fix, and what actually causes M3 forgetting:
(a) g is SHARED across all tasks and is the thing that forgot to chance (0.131) in the existing harness. PALM's defense is "g only ever sees tight anchor-snapped clusters, so interference is low" — but that is exactly the replay/regularization hope that already failed; the proposal even retains the same fragile latent replay buffer. If basins are well-separated AND mapped near-one-hot, g's job IS the oracle's job and might hold — but that is conditional on (1) and (3) working, which are shaky.
(b) THE ROUTER forgets implicitly. Adding task t+1's basin near (by farthest-point) old anchors changes the global argmin landscape: an input from task a that used to win basin a can, after a nearby basin is added, start landing closer to the new anchor under a different gain. L_sep enforces a margin on the NEW basin vs old anchors at training time, but never re-verifies that OLD task inputs still route correctly after new gains exist. There is no replay of routing decisions. So the system can have perfect phase-region retention (the s_a basin is intact) yet route old inputs to the wrong gain — the "high oracle-routed acc, low self-routed acc" failure the proposal names but does not defend against sequentially. This is precisely "separable jointly, forgets sequentially" at the ROUTER level rather than the basin level. So PALM moves the forgetting from the coupling to the router and to g, and does not prove the router survives.
critiques.will_it_stay_label_free_C2: Mechanically yes at test (no task id consumed; t* is argmin over residuals — pure geometry). But C2 is satisfied only in the trivial sense; the substantive C2 risk is that label-free routing accuracy is the real ceiling and it is untested/unbounded. The GO/NO-GO correctly centers on this (routing acc must beat 1/5 and approach the within-task-shuffle ceiling), which is the right gate. One subtle leak: mu_t is chosen by farthest-point sampling "deterministic from t" — that is label-free. But L_set/L_sep are computed per training step using the CURRENT task's label-defined batch (you know which inputs are task t during training), which is allowed under standard class-IL. No label leak there. The genuine label-free worry is the soft-routing fallback: a residual-weighted MIXTURE context fed to g can let g learn to ignore the (uninformative) context and lean on the trunk — but the trunk is frozen-random and the demo invariant (constant ctx -> chance) guards this, so a degenerate bypass via mixture is caught by the existing const-ctx control. Net: C2 is not where it dies; it dies at routing accuracy, which masquerades as a C2 success metric.
critiques.bypass_or_cheat_risk_C1_C4:
C1 (unbypassability) is preserved: trunk stays frozen-random, head comes only from g(context). Good.

C4 has two live cheats the proposal half-catches:
1. GAIN-DRIVEN DEGENERATE BASIN: L_set+L_rapid reward settling to mu_t fast. The cheapest way to minimize ||settle(x;s_t) - mu_t|| is for the dynamics to drive the pooled phase to mu_t INDEPENDENT of the input (a fixed point of J_shared+diag(s_t) near mu_t that swallows all inputs). Then context encodes the gain, not the input, and routing by residual becomes "which gain has the lowest fixed-point residual," which can be input-independent. The within-task-shuffle eval catches whether context still carries task (good), but it does NOT catch input-independence within a task. The named mitigation ("residuals must separate true-task from off-task inputs") is the right test but is not in the harness yet.
2. BEATING R6s FOR THE WRONG REASON: the R6s control freezes RANDOM connectivity but STILL trains s_t and g identically (verified: build_R6_scrambled freezes connectivity, apply_proj stays True). A diagonal gain s_t on a random-but-fixed coupling can still carve distinguishable fixed points (random projections separate points fine). So R6 might beat R6s only marginally — or R6s might nearly match R6 — because most of PALM's separability comes from the per-task gain + anchor losses, NOT from the meta-learned coupling. If R6s routes/separates almost as well as R6, the result is a NULL on the actual scientific claim (learned synchrony matters), even if absolute accuracy rises. The GO/NO-GO does require R6 > R6s, which is the correct kill-gate — but the architecture makes a null here quite likely because the coupling is barely doing the work.

No instance-memorization leak beyond what M2 already characterized (instance-level channel is ~54% of lift) — but note PALM does nothing to suppress instance-level routing, so a "win" could be instance-level argmin (the router keys on instance idiosyncrasies, not task), which would NOT generalize to test inputs and would show as train-routing >> test-routing.
critiques.bug_risk:
The single biggest implementation hazard, verified in code: THE ENTIRE CONTEXT PIPELINE IS NON-DIFFERENTIABLE AS WRITTEN. m2_hypernet._phase_context runs `with torch.no_grad()` and does `xs[layer][-1].detach().float().cpu().numpy()`, then group_directions and _pool_phase_state are pure NUMPY. PALM REQUIRES gradients to flow "through the inner Kuramoto settle into s_t and into g" (its central co-training claim) and through L_set/L_sep/L_rapid which are functions of pool_phase(state_k). To realize PALM you must rewrite group_directions + _pool_phase_state + the KLayer inner-step rollout in differentiable torch, expose the per-step state state_k (currently only the final xs[layer][-1] is used; intermediate inner steps are internal to KLayer.forward), and verify gradient parity. This is a substantial, bug-prone re-implementation, not a "two changes vs the current harness." Everything downstream (anchors, residuals, L_rapid over k=1..T) silently assumes per-inner-step differentiable phase access that the codebase does not currently provide.

Secondary bugs likely: (a) the meansq>=0 half-space mismatch (above) makes residuals never reach zero — easy to misread as "settling failed"; (b) s_t initialized to 0 means J_eff starts exactly = J_shared for ALL tasks, so before training every task's basin is identical and the router is at chance — the separation must be entirely learned, and if L_sep is weak the basins stay collapsed; (c) vectorizing 5 gain rollouts at test "cheaply" assumes the settle is cheap and that the 5 candidate states fit memory under the no-OOM constraint — the inner-step rollout with grad during training (T steps x batch) is the opposite of frugal.
critiques.salvage_or_fix:
DON'T freeze J_shared after task 0 — that is the change that most improves it AND the change that makes it actually test the thesis. The stated bet is co-training the coupling end-to-end across the stream; PALM quietly retreats to coupling-frozen-after-task-0 + per-task gain, which is the capture-then-freeze paradigm already shown insufficient. Instead: keep J_shared TRAINABLE across all tasks but protect it with a real isolation/consolidation mechanism on the COUPLING itself — e.g. a hypernet-indexed mask on the conv weights keyed by the (label-free) routed anchor, or per-task low-rank coupling deltas Delta_J_t (not just a diagonal gain) consolidated by anchored-replay of settled exemplars through the FULL dynamics. That tests whether a co-trained coupling can both separate basins AND resist overwriting — the actual C3 crux.

If you keep the frozen-J architecture, then the make-or-break single test is sequential ROUTER retention, not basin retention: after all 5 tasks, measure test-time argmin-over-gains routing accuracy on task-0 inputs (not just "does s_0 still land near mu_0" — that is trivially true by isolation). Build that into the cheapest-validation milestone NOW, because phase-region retention is guaranteed by construction and therefore proves nothing; router-survival is the only number that distinguishes PALM from a relabeled R6s. And fix the anchor geometry: sample mu_t in the REACHABLE region (mean dims free, meansq dims >=0), or change the pooled descriptor so the sphere is actually coverable, before any GPU spend.

critiques.fatal_flaws:
FLAW 1 (DECISIVE, code-contradicted). The entire "phase slot" mechanism is destroyed by the existing pooling and never reaches the hypernet. In m2_primitives.py:_pool_phase_state (lines 248-266) the context is built by pooling mean+meansq over ALL sites, where sites = B*(C//n)*H*W (h3.py:group_directions lines 490-515 reshapes (B,C,H,W)->(B, C//n, n, H,W)->(n_sites, n)). The "channel-group" axis G=C//n that the proposal calls a "slot" is EXACTLY the axis that gets averaged away. n is the SPHERE dimension (n=4 = points on S^3), NOT a per-task capacity knob. So "expand from 64ch/4 groups to 160ch/20 groups" leaves the context dimension at 2n=8 regardless (context_dim_for_n in theta_generator.py = 2*n). Widening C only adds more sites to pool over; it does not add context dimensions. The proposal's premise -- "n=4 is too small to carry H(T), expand the oscillator so C_ctx scales with K" -- conflates n (sphere dim, fixed by AKOrN geometry) with C//n (group/site count, already pooled out). The "concatenation of all slots' 2n-per-slot descriptors" is NOT what _phase_context computes; it would require a wholesale rewrite of the pooling to be per-channel-block, which the proposal never specifies and which would discard the site-count/permutation invariance the descriptor was built for. As written, PSA's slots are invisible to g.

FLAW 2. Even granting a per-slot pool rewrite, the capacity argument is wrong. To make C_ctx scale you do NOT need to expand the oscillator at all -- you could just widen n or change the pooling. Expanding to 20 groups and freezing coupling columns is an enormous, fragile intervention to buy a few extra context dims you could get for free. The diagnosis "M2 0.24 is capacity-starved" is unsupported: the ORACLE one-hot context (clean, non-colliding, 5-dim, drift-free BY CONSTRUCTION) only reaches ~0.52 in this exact harness (m3_oracle logs: 0.517-0.545). That is the true ceiling and it is NOT capacity-limited (5 one-hot bits trivially exceed H(5 tasks)=2.32 bits). The bottleneck is the FROZEN-RANDOM TRUNK: the head g(c)->theta is linear in features, applied to task-agnostic random features, so even a perfect context caps at ~0.52. PSA cannot exceed the oracle, and the oracle is already far below "solved." The headline target of 0.40-0.50 is BELOW the oracle the harness already achieves with a one-hot -- PSA at best reproduces the oracle while adding massive machinery.

FLAW 3. The "provable zero drift" claim is false even on its own terms. The proposal admits cross-slot READS are allowed ("slot-B coupling may READ slot-A channels"). In AKOrN the L1 phase is a fixed-point of a recurrent dynamic (T steps) over the FULL coupling Conv2d. Slot-A's converged phase is a function of the joint dynamics; if B's columns change how energy flows through shared positions and the readout/omega scaffolding, the attractor basin slot-A's input lands in can shift even if A's own output weights are frozen. "Reading A cannot change A" is only true for a single feedforward pass; for an iterated Kuramoto fixed point with shared normalization and shared omega, it is not. The proposal hand-waves this as "harmless."
critiques.will_it_forget_C3: Partly, but with an unproven core and a relocated failure. The hard-freeze of coupling output-columns + per-slot GroupNorm DOES stop the most obvious g/coupling overwrite, IF (big if) slots were real context dimensions -- which Flaw 1 shows they are not. The "provable zero drift" is overstated: AKOrN L1 is an iterated fixed point over the shared coupling Conv2d with shared omega and (per the BACKBONE config) global_omg=True; cross-slot reads + shared scaffolding mean B's training can perturb the basin slot-A's input converges to, so "(input->slot-A phase) is a fixed function for all t>=A" is not established for a recurrent oscillator, only for a feedforward map. More importantly, C3 is not actually the binding constraint here: the oracle one-hot already shows ~0.02 forgetting in this harness, so the harness's g-forgetting is solvable with a clean context. PSA converts forgetting into ROUTING error (its own stated main failure mode). So even in the best case it does not "stop forgetting"; it trades it for label-free recall error that it then cannot bound except against the same 0.52 oracle.
critiques.will_it_stay_label_free_C2: At test, nominally yes -- the router gate is a function of the input/trunk feature alone, no task id consumed at inference. But there is a serious C2 dependency: the router is the ONLY thing standing between PSA and chance, and it is trained with the task label (allowed at train time) on top of a FROZEN-RANDOM trunk feature. If the frozen-random trunk does not linearly separate the 5 tasks, the router cannot route, and PSA collapses to chance -- the proposal concedes this. So C2 is satisfied formally but the whole result hinges on "can a fixed random projection separate the tasks," which is an untested empirical bet and, if it succeeds strongly, raises a C1/C4 question (see below): if the trunk feature alone separates tasks well enough to route, the channel is doing little work. Also the 16-sample/slot latent buffer of (c, feats, y) re-fits the router gate -- benign for labels at train time, but it is a replay buffer in all but name and must be reported as such.
critiques.bypass_or_cheat_risk_C1_C4: Real and structural. (a) The router creates a NEW task-adaptive pathway that partially BYPASSES the phase channel: the router reads the trunk feature, decides the slot, and gates the context -- so task-discriminative information now flows trunk->router->gate->c, not only phase->c. If the router does the separating and g just reads a near-one-hot gate, PSA has quietly rebuilt the "static task-adaptive alternative" that C1 forbids. The decisive control must be: does PSA beat a baseline where the SAME router gate (trunk->slot) feeds g a LEARNED per-slot embedding with NO oscillator at all (i.e., replace phase descriptors with trainable slot vectors). If that ablation matches PSA, the oscillator is decorative and C1 is violated in spirit. The proposal's oracle-router diagnostic does the opposite of what's needed -- it removes router error to credit the slots, but never removes the SLOTS to test whether the router alone carries it. (b) C4: PSA must beat R6s (frozen-random slot coupling) -- but R6s gets the SAME router and SAME slot allocation/freeze, so if the router carries the task, R6s will track R6 and the "learned coupling" claim dies, exactly as the M2 replay result already evaporated (research-log 2026-06-05). (c) Per-task slot allocation with task-label-driven router CE is close to storing task identity in the gate weights = a soft task-id memory, skirting C2.
critiques.bug_risk: (1) The headline bug is conceptual-as-code: masking the connectivity Conv2d "output columns" does not produce per-slot phase descriptors because _phase_context pools over the group/site axis -- any implementation that "adds a channel-slot mask + 1-line masked-coupling step" (as the cheapest-validation section claims) will run green and silently produce the SAME 2n-dim pooled context for every slot, so the orthogonality loss <c_active, c_s'> operates on collapsed vectors and the router gates a context that does not actually vary by slot. This will LOOK like it works (no crash) while testing nothing. (2) requires_grad_(False) on coupling columns does not stop Adam from updating them if their momentum buffers persist and grads are nonzero via shared parameters; per-task optimizer state reset is mentioned only for g, not for the coupling optimizer -- frozen columns can still drift through shared-parameter aliasing in a single Conv2d weight tensor unless the mask is applied to the GRADIENT every step, not just requires_grad. (3) Per-slot GroupNorm "freeze affine" does not freeze the normalization itself if groups are not actually disjoint per slot -- GroupNorm groups are fixed by C//n at construction and may not align to slot boundaries. (4) global_omg=True (BACKBONE) means omega is shared across all channels/slots; "frozen-random omega scaffolding" is shared state that couples slots, contradicting the disjointness claim.
critiques.salvage_or_fix: Drop the oscillator-expansion/slot story entirely and confront the real ceiling. The single most clarifying experiment, runnable in the existing harness in ~1 GPU-hour with NO slot machinery: raise the oracle's effective head capacity by making the TRUNK partially trainable or richer, and measure the new ceiling. If the oracle one-hot context cannot beat ~0.52 even with a better head, then NO context channel (phase, slot, or otherwise) can solve this testbed and the whole PSA premise is moot -- you are optimizing toward a ceiling set by the frozen-random trunk, not by context capacity. If a richer head lifts the oracle to ~0.8, THEN the question "can co-trained phase approach oracle" becomes worth asking -- and the minimal honest version is: co-train coupling J end-to-end with g under EWC/Fisher protection on the coupling (standard, well-understood) and compare to R6s, WITHOUT inventing slots or a separate router. Critically, before any of this, fix or replace _pool_phase_state so the context dimension can actually grow (e.g., a small learned linear pool that preserves a per-channel-block readout), and re-verify the M2 channel still exists under the new pool. Until the pooling collapses are fixed, PSA's slots are a no-op and the validation milestone would produce a false GO.

synthesis.chosen_design:
NAME: PALR — Phase-Attention Learned Readout with grown low-rank coupling (one design, fixes grafted from PALM/PALACE/PSA).

WHY THIS DESIGN (the trade-off): The artifacts already FALSIFY the two legs every other proposal leans on. (1) step4b (n=8): co-training a contrastive/separation objective on the LIVE phase does NOT raise the task channel (task_lift 0.124->0.121, decode 0.430->0.432) — so "shape the dynamics apart" is dead (kills PALACE-L2, PhaseLock-supcon, PALM-meta-settle premise). (2) step4a (n=5/arm): label-free task-decode from the frozen-J pooled phase is R6 mlp_cv 0.294 == R6s 0.297, only +0.08 over the 0.216 shuffle floor — the selection signal is DEAD and coupling-AGNOSTIC (kills PALACE-router, PSA-router, PALM-energy-argmin as load-bearing). The ONE component present in EVERY null experiment, never itself varied, is the fixed 2n=8 permutation/site-invariant MEAN-POOL (m2_primitives._pool_phase_state: mean+meansq over ALL sites, collapsing the C//n group axis that carries which-pair/relational binding). The oracle proves a clean 5-bit context routes to 0.524 through the SAME tiny generator+frozen trunk; the phase channel caps at ~0.24 task-level. So the untested, highest-leverage, lowest-bug-risk lever is the READOUT FROM phase, co-trained, not the dynamics and not a frozen-phase selector. PALR attacks exactly that, and grafts the only C3 mechanism that survives the C3 critique without a label-free selector: structural parameter isolation (grow+freeze).

ARCHITECTURE (drop-in to m2_hypernet.py; trunk/oracle/within-shuffle/dP5 harness unchanged):
- FROZEN ALWAYS (C1 unbypassability): the random task-agnostic trunk (nn.Sequential conv stack -> 64-d features f). The generated head theta is the ONLY task-adaptive route to logits. Unchanged from M2.
- TRAINED + co-trained END-TO-END (the new bet): (a) the AKOrN coupling J = KLayer.connectivity convs across L layers (R6 arm); (b) a NEW differentiable LEARNED POOL P: a small attention-pool that reads the FULL L1 phase field xs[1][-1] (B,C,H,W) and emits a context c of dim d_ctx (start d_ctx=16, i.e. 2x the old 8). P = a 1x1 conv scorer over the C//n group-direction map producing K=8 attention maps, each softmax-normalized over sites, used to weight-pool the per-group unit directions -> K pooled n-vectors concatenated and projected to d_ctx. This REPLACES the fixed mean-pool and PRESERVES relational/group structure the mean-pool destroys; (c) the hypernet g = PhaseContextThetaGen (d_ctx -> theta). Co-training requires the differentiable torch port of group_directions+pool (see first_build).
- R6s CONTROL: identical pipeline, J = frozen-random connectivity (build_R6_scrambled); P and g still train. Isolates whether LEARNED coupling (not just the learned pool) carries the channel.
- CL PROTOCOL (class-IL, 5 tasks, labels at train only): Task 0: co-train {J, P, g, low-rank-delta dJ_0, gen-column g_0} with CE only. Then HARD-FREEZE the shared backbone J and the shared pool P. Task t>=1: instantiate a fresh per-task low-rank coupling delta dJ_t = U_t V_t^T (rank r=4, added to L1 connectivity output) and a fresh per-task generator column g_t (one extra hidden row in g.fc1 + its fan-out); FREEZE all dJ_{<t}, g_{<t}; train ONLY {dJ_t, g_t} on current-task data + a small raw-exemplar replay buffer (CCC-legal re-feeding) through the LIVE pipeline.
- LABEL-FREE ROUTING (C2): a router head R reads the co-trained context c (NOT the dead frozen-J descriptor) -> soft distribution w over the t allocated per-task deltas/columns. R is supervised by the current task id at TRAIN time (class-IL legal); at TEST it routes from input alone via c. Injected knob = sum_k w_k {dJ_k, g_k}. CRITICAL: train with teacher-forced one-hot AND with the router's own soft w mixed in (anneal) so the soft-mix operating point is in-distribution (fixes PALACE's train/test knob mismatch).
- LOSSES: L = CE(g_route(c)->head applied to trunk(x), y) + lambda_route*CE(R(c), task_id) + lambda_replay*CE on raw replayed exemplars through live pipeline. lambda_route=1.0, lambda_replay=1.0, buffer=20 raw images/task. NO contrastive/separation term (step4b proved it null — deliberately omitted to avoid re-running a dead mechanism).
- ANTI-FORGETTING (C3, survives the critique): purely STRUCTURAL parameter-isolation, NOT regularization (EWC-in-phase was the wrong side of the CCC bound per the PhaseLock critique). After task t, dJ_t and g_t get requires_grad_(False); task t+1's optimizer physically cannot move them. The shared J and shared pool P are frozen after task 0 so the substrate that defines all basins never drifts. Forgetting of the head/dynamics is structurally ~0; the ONLY residual failure surface is ROUTER mis-routing, which is measured directly and protected by tiny raw replay (the load-bearing, CCC-legal force).
synthesis.staged_milestones:
M0 (CHEAPEST, 1-2 GPU-hr, 1 seed, shapes — THE GATE): Build ONLY the differentiable torch pool port + the LEARNED attention-pool P, drop into run_joint (JOINT upper bound, NO CL, NO grow/freeze yet). Unit-test the torch pool matches numpy group_directions+_pool_phase_state to 1e-4 AND that grad flows to J and P. Then JOINT co-train {J, P, g} and measure within-task-shuffle TASK-level lift vs the locked baseline (R6 fixed-pool task-lift ~0.141). GO if learned-pool R6 task-level (within-shuffle) lift >= +0.08 over the fixed-pool R6 baseline (i.e. >= ~0.22) AND R6 beats R6s by a clear paired margin (>= +0.05) AND const-ctx stays ~chance (no bypass). NO-GO if learned pool ties fixed pool or R6 ties R6s — that means the pooling was NOT the bottleneck and the whole co-training bet is exhausted on this construct (-> kill_criteria).

M1 (only if M0 GO; 2-3 GPU-hr, 3 seeds, shapes): Add the label-free ROUTER R on the co-trained context c (single shared substrate, joint, no grow yet). Measure label-free router slot-accuracy on held-out test inputs (chance 0.20). GO if router slot-acc >= 0.45 AND R6 router-acc > R6s router-acc (paired). This is the C2 make-or-break: it tests whether the LEARNED pool's context (unlike the dead frozen-J descriptor) carries a selectable task signal. NO-GO -> the channel is usable only when told the task (oracle-routed), label-free recall fails -> escalate to M1b (richer router/larger d_ctx, ONE pre-set bump) then kill if still null.

M2 (only if M1 GO; 3-4 GPU-hr, 3 seeds, shapes): Add grow+freeze (per-task dJ_t, g_t) + tiny raw replay -> the FULL sequential PALR. Measure final_acc, forgetting, AND label-free-routed final_acc vs oracle-routed final_acc, vs the locked naive baseline (final ~0.131) and oracle ceiling (~0.524). GO if PALR-R6 label-free final_acc >= 0.40 with forgetting < 0.10 AND beats PALR-R6s by >= 0.10 (paired) AND dP5 strongly negative (channel real, no bypass).

M3 (only if M2 GO; ~6-8 GPU-hr, 5 seeds): n=5 confirmation on shapes with EXACT paired sign-flip test, then GENERALIZATION to SplitCIFAR-10 (the harness already mirrors run_joint to CIFAR). GO = paired exact p<0.05 R6>R6s on shapes AND directionally consistent on CIFAR. This is the headline-worthy result.
synthesis.preregistered_ablations:
All run with IDENTICAL code/config (only the ablated knob changes), paired by seed, EXACT sign-flip 2-sided test for headline calls (the project's locked standard):
1. R6 vs R6s (C4 PRIMARY): full PALR pipeline, learned coupling vs frozen-random coupling co-trained identically. Pre-registered margin: R6 final_acc - R6s final_acc >= 0.10 AND R6 within-shuffle task-lift > R6s. If R6s matches R6, the win is the learned pool + grow/freeze (a hypernet/progressive-net trick), NOT learned synchrony -> scientific NULL on the thesis, report honestly.
2. ORACLE ceiling: one-hot true task id as context (existing --oracle-ctx path) must reproduce ~0.524 final / ~0.02 forgetting in the SAME run as a liveness check; PALR cannot exceed it (it is the cap).
3. LABEL-FREE test (C2): label-free-routed final_acc vs ORACLE-ROUTED final_acc (gate = true task one-hot into the SAME grown columns). Gap = router error; report both. Label-free must clear naive (0.131) by a wide margin or C2 fails.
4. DEGENERATE-BASELINE firewall (graft from PALM critique): replace the oscillator entirely with the SAME router + learned per-slot embeddings (no phase, no J). If this matches PALR, the oscillator is decorative and C1 is violated in spirit -> NULL.
5. POOL ablation: learned attention-pool vs the fixed mean-pool, both under full PALR. Isolates how much of any gain is the readout (the lever this design bets on) vs the dynamics.
6. NO-NEW-MECHANISM ablation: PALR with grow+freeze+replay but WITHOUT the learned pool (fixed mean-pool) vs full PALR. If they tie, the pool was not the lever.
7. INSTANCE-LEAK guard: report router/final acc ONLY on test inputs whose class has ZERO exemplar in the replay buffer (kills the 54%-instance-leak cheat the n=20 decomposition exposed).
8. WITHIN-TASK-SHUFFLE + const-ctx + wrong-ctx dP5 invariants (existing harness) must all hold (task-level, not instance; no bypass; channel used).
synthesis.kill_criteria:
STOP and accept the M3 negative for good if ANY of these fire (each is a pre-registered, adversarially-checked gate, not a config-shop):
1. M0 NO-GO: the LEARNED differentiable attention-pool, co-trained end-to-end with J, does NOT lift the within-task-shuffle TASK-level channel >= +0.08 over the fixed-pool baseline (~0.141), OR R6 ties R6s through it (paired). This is the decisive test: it means the bottleneck is NOT the pooling/readout but is genuinely ARCHITECTURAL/in-the-dynamics (consistent with step4b null), and since "shape the dynamics" is already falsified (step4b) and "better readout on frozen phase" is already falsified (M2 contrastive-readout null), BOTH the readout side and the dynamics side are then exhausted -> the phase channel's modest ~0.24 task capacity on this construct is a real ceiling, not an effort problem. ACCEPT the oracle-validated M3 negative.
2. M1 NO-GO after the single pre-set router/d_ctx bump: label-free router slot-acc stays at chance-vs-control on the co-trained context too -> the label-free selection signal is genuinely absent (C2 unachievable), confirming step4a generalizes even to a learned pool. ACCEPT negative.
3. C4 falsification at any stage: PALR-R6s matches PALR-R6 within noise (paired, n>=5) -> any gain is progressive-net/learned-pool plumbing, not learned synchrony -> the THESIS (learned synchrony as the channel) is falsified even if accuracy rose. Report as "hypernet capacity bypasses forgetting, but synchrony is not the mechanism" — an honest negative for the synchrony thesis.
4. Degenerate-baseline (ablation 4) matches PALR -> oscillator decorative, C1 violated in spirit -> negative.
Hard scope cap to prevent p-hacking: ONE pre-set value per hyperparameter (lambda_route, lambda_replay, rank r, K, d_ctx) declared before any GPU run; if M0 fails, exactly ONE d_ctx/K bump is allowed, then stop. No sweeping for a flattering config (the project's repeatedly-burned failure mode, in both directions).
synthesis.first_build:
In m2_hypernet.py, write a differentiable torch replacement for _phase_context that the WHOLE design depends on. Concretely add two functions and one module:

1. `torch_group_directions(state, n)`: torch port of h3.group_directions for a (B,C,H,W) tensor -> (B, G, n, H*W) where G=C//n, L2-normalized over the n-axis with clamp(min=1e-12). Must replicate the SAME fixed-index site subsample (use the same default_rng(0) indices materialized once) so the descriptor and its gradient match numpy. UNIT TEST in this conversation: assert torch output (detached) matches np group_directions+_pool_phase_state to <1e-4 on a fixed random (2,64,8,8) tensor, AND assert grad flows (state.requires_grad_(True); loss=out.sum(); loss.backward(); assert state.grad is not None and finite).

2. `class LearnedPhasePool(nn.Module)`: input the (B,G,n,HW) group-direction tensor; a 1x1-conv-equivalent scorer (nn.Linear over the n-axis pooled features, or nn.Conv2d on the G-map) producing K=8 attention logits per site; softmax over the HW site axis; weight-pool the per-group unit directions -> K pooled n-vectors; concat -> nn.Linear to d_ctx=16. Returns c (B, d_ctx). This REPLACES the mean-pool. Keep it tiny (<5k params).

3. Rewrite `_phase_context` to (a) DROP `torch.no_grad()` but KEEP h3_seeded(0) so the random oscillator init x=randn_like(c) is FIXED per identical input (the determinism bug the PhaseLock critique flagged — c(x) must be stable batch-to-batch or every downstream loss anchors a moving target), (b) call akorn.net.feature(x) WITH grad, (c) take xs[1][-1], (d) torch_group_directions -> LearnedPhasePool -> c.

Then change run_joint's `_cache` to NOT precompute/freeze context (it must be live+differentiable now) and add J, P to the optimizer alongside g. This single differentiable-pool build is the load-bearing, highest-bug-risk change (the entire co-training claim and M0 gate ride on it); everything else (grow/freeze, router, replay) is additive and only built after M0 GO. Validate the unit test BEFORE any GPU spend — a green run with a wrong-grad pool would produce a false GO.
synthesis.one_line_summary: PALR co-trains a small differentiable LEARNED attention-pool over the L1 phase field (the one component every prior null shared but never varied) end-to-end with coupling J and hypernet g, routes label-free via that learned context, and bypasses forgetting by structurally growing+freezing per-task low-rank coupling deltas and generator columns — gated by a single cheapest test (does the learned pool lift the within-shuffle task channel >=+0.08 and beat R6s?), with an honest kill if pooling is not the bottleneck.
